# Spec: Breath Cycle and Ice/Water/Gas Phase States

## Purpose

Every entity in the Coherence Network — ideas, specs, concepts — exists somewhere in a natural lifecycle: crystallized and stable (Ice), actively flowing and changing (Water), or speculative and exploratory (Gas). The binary `validated/not-validated` flag that ideas currently carry cannot represent this richness. This spec adds a phase state layer and breath cycle event log to every entity type, making lifecycle visible at a glance and traceable over time. The phase indicator on every card makes the body's state legible without needing to open the detail view; the breath cycle timeline on the idea detail page shows how an entity has moved through its lifecycle.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — PhaseState enum**: Three states — `ice` (stable, archived, reference; no active iteration expected), `water` (active, flowing, changing; the working state), `gas` (speculative, volatile, experimental; early exploration). Default for new entities: `water`.

- [ ] **R2 — BreathStep enum**: Four breath steps — `inhale` (gather, expand, take in new material), `hold` (integrate, sit with what has arrived), `exhale` (release, publish, ship), `rest` (observe, sense what remains). Steps are timestamped events appended to the entity's breath cycle log; they do not gate phase transitions.

- [ ] **R3 — Phase API (GET/PATCH)**: `GET /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/phase` returns current phase, current breath step (last recorded), and `updated_at`. `PATCH /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/phase` accepts `{"phase": "ice"|"water"|"gas", "reason": "optional string"}` and persists the transition, recording a `BreathCycleEvent`.

- [ ] **R4 — Breath cycle API (GET/POST)**: `GET /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/breath-cycle` returns the ordered event log (newest first). `POST /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/breath-cycle` appends a breath step event with `{"step": "inhale"|"hold"|"exhale"|"rest", "note": "optional string"}`.

- [ ] **R5 — Entity type scope**: `entity_type` accepts `idea`, `spec`, `concept`. Unknown types → 422 Unprocessable Entity with a clear message listing valid types.

- [ ] **R6 — Phase indicator (web)**: `PhaseIndicator` component renders a small icon inline: ❄ (snowflake) for ice, ~ (wave) for water, ☁ (cloud) for gas, plus a color-coded label. Used on `IdeaCard` and on the idea detail page header.

- [ ] **R7 — Breath cycle visualization (web)**: `BreathCycleViz` component on the idea detail page renders the breath step timeline as a compact vertical list of timestamped events (step icon, step name, note, timestamp). Reads from `GET /api/entities/idea/{id}/breath-cycle`.

- [ ] **R8 — CLI**: `coh phase <entity-type> <entity-id>` prints current phase and last breath step. `coh phase set <entity-type> <entity-id> <phase>` PATCHes the phase. Both commands live under the existing `coh` CLI binary.

- [ ] **R9 — Persistence**: Two new tables — `entity_phases` (one row per entity, upserted on PATCH) and `breath_cycle_events` (append-only event log). Both use String columns for portability across SQLite (dev/test) and PostgreSQL (prod).

## API Contract

### `GET /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/phase`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "entity_type": "idea",
  "entity_id": "agent-pipeline",
  "phase": "water",
  "last_step": "inhale",
  "step_note": "gathering feedback from first contributors",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00Z"
}
```

**Response 404** — entity phase record not found (entity may not have an explicit phase set yet; return default `water` with `updated_at: null` instead of 404 for better UX).

### `PATCH /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/phase`

**Request body**
```json
{
  "phase": "ice",
  "reason": "concept is stable and referenced by 3 specs"
}
```

**Response 200** — same shape as GET response above, with updated `phase` and `updated_at`.

**Response 422** — invalid phase value or unknown entity_type.

### `GET /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/breath-cycle`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "entity_type": "idea",
  "entity_id": "agent-pipeline",
  "events": [
    {
      "id": "evt_abc123",
      "event_type": "step",
      "step": "exhale",
      "note": "shipped v1 to prod",
      "actor_id": "contributor_xyz",
      "timestamp": "2026-04-25T09:00:00Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "evt_def456",
      "event_type": "phase_transition",
      "phase": "water",
      "reason": "now actively implementing",
      "actor_id": "contributor_xyz",
      "timestamp": "2026-04-20T08:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### `POST /api/entities/{entity_type}/{id}/breath-cycle`

**Request body**
```json
{
  "step": "inhale",
  "note": "reading new external signal"
}
```

**Response 201**
```json
{
  "id": "evt_ghi789",
  "event_type": "step",
  "step": "inhale",
  "note": "reading new external signal",
  "actor_id": null,
  "timestamp": "2026-04-25T10:01:00Z"
}
```

## Data Model

```yaml
EntityPhase (tab
```

# Spec: CC flow to creators on asset use

> **Idea id**: `cc-flow-on-asset-use` (registry key for tasks; may sit under value-attribution in the ideas tree).
> **Related super-ideas**: [value-attribution](../ideas/value-attribution.md), [coherence-credit](../ideas/coherence-credit.md)
> **Related specs** (must stay coherent): [assets-api](assets-api.md), [value-lineage-and-payout-attribution](value-lineage-and-payout-attribution.md), [asset-renderer-plugin](asset-renderer-plugin.md), [distribution-engine](distribution-engine.md), [cc-economics-and-value-coherence](cc-economics-and-value-coherence.md), [story-protocol-integration](story-protocol-integration.md) (x402 + usage events), [creator-economy-promotion](creator-economy-promotion.md) (proof cards, featured assets)

## Summary

Coherence Credit should flow to asset creators and contributors when their work is *used*: viewed in the browser, fetched over the API, or incorporated into a downstream build with recorded evidence. Today, render events and CC splits exist, and value-lineage supports generic usage events — but the **end-to-end chain** (which asset, which contributor, which view, how much CC, and how to prove it over time) is not a single, observable product path. This spec requires **unified usage events**, **creator-visible proof**, and **lineage-consistent attribution** so we can see whether the loop is working and make that proof **clearer as data accumulates** (dashboards, time series, and lineage drill-downs — not one-off numbers buried in logs).

## Purpose

- Turn “every read is signal” into **fair, explainable** CC movement toward creators, including **micro-attribution** for light engagement.
- Make **3D/blueprint-style** reuse measurable: when a builder posts implementation evidence referencing an asset, that event **weights** attribution above a passive view.
- Give operators and contributors **falsifiable** checks: the system can show **zero** (with reason: no lineage link, no auth, rate limit) or **non-zero** with a reconciliation trail.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 (usage definition)**: A **usage event** is any durable signal that a specific asset was consumed: (a) `POST /api/render-events` with a `RenderEventCreate` from the web client after a render session; (b) an authenticated or rate-limited **server-side** log when `GET /api/assets/{id}` (or the canonical string-id route) returns 200; (c) a distinct **evidence** event when implementation/build evidence is recorded and references an `asset_id`. Each event type is mapped to a consistent internal shape (`asset_id`, `consumer_id` or `reader_id`, `source`, `metric`, `value`, `optional lineage_id`).

- [ ] **R2 (lineage linkage)**: For assets that participate in a value-lineage link, new usage and render-sourced data **must** be able to append to `POST /api/value-lineage/links/{lineage_id}/usage-events` (or a documented bridge that does so with the same `UsageEvent` semantics) so `GET .../valuation` and payout preview see increased `measured_value_total` / `event_count` when appropriate. If no lineage id exists, the system records the event and exposes **why** lineage payout is N/A in creator/operator UIs (no silent failure).

- [ ] **R3 (CC amounts)**: Render path keeps **cc_pool = duration_ms × BASE_CC_RATE** behavior (or successor formula in config, versioned) and the **80/15/5** (or override) split. API-only “view” events use a **separate, documented** micro-CC or weight table so a raw GET is not a free full payout farm; values are **capped** and **tested**.

- [ ] **R4 (evidence / build)**: Storing or linking “implementation evidence” (e.g. blueprint used in a build) produces a `usage` or `value` event with **higher weight** than a single page view, and appears in the same per-asset analytics and lineage valuation where applicable. Exact API shape can extend existing contribution or evidence routes listed in ## Files.

- [ ] **R5 (observability — is it working?)**: Expose at least: (1) per-asset aggregates already aligned with [creator-economy-promotion](creator-economy-promotion.md) and render analytics; (2) a **“CC flow health”** JSON for an asset or lineage (e.g. counts, last event time, lineage linked or not, settlement batch id if any) so operators and automated monitors can as
```

*(truncated at 8KB — fetch full content via `git fetch origin fbb6196d689df4c8053608c7c6a6faa1d3138ae8 && git show fbb6196d689df4c8053608c7c6a6faa1d3138ae8:specs/cc-flow-on-asset-use.md`)*

## ci-noise-reduction

- **Spec file**: `specs/ci-noise-reduction.md`
- **Canonical attempt**: branch `task/task_fe488f9d0ef`, tip `4df5ab5a`, 2026-03-31

### Content

```markdown
# Spec: CI Noise Reduction — Scheduled Workflows to Daily, Failure-Only Notifications

## Purpose

The CI pipeline was generating ~44 runs/day across scheduled workflows, creating notification
fatigue and making real failures invisible. This spec covers:

1. Consolidating scheduled CI runs from per-push + per-schedule to daily-only
2. Configuring GitHub notification settings to send email only on failures
3. Disabling PR push notifications that fire on every commit
4. Adding a status badge so the current CI state is always visible at a glance

After this change the target is ≤12 CI runs/day with zero noise on green runs.

---

## Current State

`.github/workflows/` contains multiple workflow files. Common sources of excess runs:
- `on: push` triggers on every commit to main (including merge commits from runner)
- `on: pull_request` triggers on every PR commit (runner creates many small PRs)
- Some workflows have both `push` and `schedule` triggers, doubling the run count
- GitHub email notifications default to "Participating and @mentions" which includes every CI run

Estimated breakdown of 44 runs/day:
- ~20 from runner-merged PRs (each triggers `on: push` on main)
- ~14 from PR commits (runner pushes feature branches)
- ~10 from scheduled jobs

---

## Required Changes

### 1. Audit `.github/workflows/*.yml` — move to daily schedule

For each workflow that runs on `push` to `main` AND has a non-critical purpose
(e.g., dependency scans, code quality, coverage reports), change:

```yaml
# Before
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 */6 * * *'  # every 6 hours

# After
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # once daily at 6am UTC
  workflow_dispatch:       # keep manual trigger
```

Keep `on: push` ONLY for:
- `test.yml` (unit + integration tests) — must run on every PR merge
- `deploy.yml` (production deploy) — triggered by merge to main

### 2. PR workflows — skip on runner-generated branches

Add a filter so workflows don't trigger on runner's `worker/` branches:

```yaml
on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths-ignore:
      - 'specs/**'
      - '*.md'
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths-ignore:
      - 'specs/**'
      - '*.md'
```

### 3. GitHub notification settings (user-level, documented in RUNBOOK)

Add to `docs/RUNBOOK.md` under "CI Setup":
```
GitHub notification tuning (one-time per contributor):
  Settings → Notifications → GitHub Actions:
    - Email: "Only failures, cancellations, and requests to approve"
    - Web: "Send notifications for failed workflows only"
  Settings → Notifications → Pull Requests:
    - "Only notify me for pull requests that need my review"
```

### 4. README status badge

Add to `README.md` after the project header:
```markdown
[![CI](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/actions/workflows/test.yml)
```

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.github/workflows/*.yml` | Remove `on: push` from non-critical workflows; add `workflow_dispatch` |
| `.github/workflows/test.yml` | Add `paths-ignore` for `specs/**` and `*.md` |
| `docs/RUNBOOK.md` | Add GitHub notification tuning instructions |
| `README.md` | Add CI status badge |

---

## Verification Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Run count drops after change
- **Setup**: Merge 5 commits to main over 1 hour
- **Action**: Check `gh run list --limit 20 --json status,name,createdAt`
- **Expected**: Only `test.yml` triggered (not dependency scan, not coverage report)
- **Edge**: `workflow_dispatch` still allows manual trigger of any workflow

### Scenario 2: Spec-only commits don't trigger tests
- **Setup**: Merge a commit that only changes `specs/` files
- **Action**: Observe GitHub Actions tab
- **Expected**: No test workflow triggered (paths-ignore filter)
- **Edge**: A commit touching both `specs/` and `api/` does trigger tests

### Scenario 3: Daily scheduled jobs still run
- **Action**: Check `gh run list --workflow=dependency-scan.yml --limit 5`
- **Expected**: One run per day, at 06:00 UTC

### Scenario 4: Status badge visible
- **Action**: Open `README.md` on GitHub
- **Expected**: Green CI badge visible in the header
- **Edge**: Badge shows "failing" correctly when tests are broken

### Scenario 5: Total daily runs ≤ 12
- **Setup**: Normal day of runner activity (10–20 PR merges)
- **Action**: `gh run list --created $(date +%Y-%m-%d) --json name | python3 -c "import sys,json; r=json.load(sys.stdin); print(len(r))"`
- **Expected**: Count ≤ 12

---

## Risks and Assumptions

- **Critical workflows**: `test.yml` and `deploy.yml` must keep `on: push` — removing them
  would mean broken code reaches main undetected.
- **Existing workflow names**: Some workflows may be referenced by other jobs; changing
  triggers must not break `needs:` dependencies.
- **Notification settings are per-user**: The RUNBOOK documents them; they cannot be
  enforced org-wide on free GitHub plan.
- **`paths-ignore` and required status checks**: If `test.yml` is a required status check
  for PR merges, skipping it on spec-only commits may block merges. Use `paths-ignore`
  with care or add a bypass job.

---

## Known Gaps and Follow-up Tasks

- **Concurrency limits**: Add `concurrency: ci-${{ github.ref }}` to cancel in-progress
  runs when a new push supersedes them — reduces wasted runner minutes.
- **Runner-generated PRs**: The runner creates many PRs for small ideas; consider batching
  small spec tasks to reduce PR volume at the source.
- **Cost tracking**: GitHub Actions minutes are limited; add a monthly usage check to
  `docs/RUNBOOK.md`.
```

---

    symbols: [jobs.validate-thread-process, steps."Detect changed surfaces", steps."Validate commit evidence", steps."Validate spec quality contract"]

  - "Changed-surface detection is unified so local preflight matches CI behavior."
  - "CI emits actionable failure reasons and points to the exact remediation command."

  - "Local `scripts/worktree_pr_guard.py --mode local --base-ref origin/main` predicts CI outcomes for the same diff (within documented exceptions)."
  - "Change Contract workflow produces a machine-readable `contract_report.json` artifact and fails only on true contract failure (no false negatives from in-flight checks)."
  - "Verification scenarios in this spec can be run and match expected outputs."

  - "No new CI features outside the contract described here (keep deltas tiny)."
  - "Do not add new secrets or require privileged GitHub permissions beyond what existing workflows already use."
  - "No environment-variable fallbacks in runtime application code (CI scripts/workflows may use inputs/vars as they already do)."
---

# Spec: CI Pipeline (ci-pipeline)

## Summary

Define the **CI pipeline contract** for Coherence Network so that local preflight, GitHub Actions “Thread Gates”, and post-merge “Change Contract” behave as one coherent system. This spec is focused on **commit-evidence enforcement**, **spec-quality enforcement**, and **predictable surfaced-based gating** (API/web/runtime) with actionable failure output.

> Note: `ideas/ci-pipeline.md` is not present in this repository worktree (only the parent `ideas/data-infrastructure.md` exists). This spec treats `ci-pipeline` as an idea-id used by commit-evidence tracking and CI contracts, and records assumptions/risks accordingly.

## Purpose

- Prevent silent regressions where CI passes but local gates fail (or vice-versa).
- Make “what blocks a PR” explicit and deterministic: **Thread Gates** for PR-time checks, **Change Contract** for post-merge contract completion/acknowledgement.
- Ensure commit-evidence tracking can reliably drive automation (routing, contribution acknowledgement, and audits).

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Thread Gates must enforce commit-evidence contract (policy-explicit).**
  - **Behavior**: For pull requests (excluding dependabot), CI must validate that at least one `docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_*.json` changed in the PR diff and that evidence validates for the diff range.
  - **Policy**: This requirement must specify whether failures are **blocking** or **non-blocking**. If non-blocking, CI must still surface a structured warning and link to remediation.
  - **Files**:
    - `.github/workflows/thread-gates.yml` — make the enforcement policy explicit (block or warn).
    - `scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py` — ensure stable output strings for CI parsing (“OK:” / “ERROR:”).

- [ ] **R2 — Local preflight must match CI gating decisions for the same change-set.**
  - **Behavior**: Running `python3 scripts/worktree_pr_guard.py --mode local --base-ref origin/main` should (a) run the same guards as CI for the impacted surfaces, and (b) fail for the same reasons CI would fail, except where explicitly documented (for example: hosted deployment-health checks when local-only mode is enabled).
  - **Files**:
    - `scripts/worktree_pr_guard.py` — unify “changed surfaces” detection and guard order with CI.
    - `.github/workflows/thread-gates.yml` — keep changed-surface rules aligned with local scope predicates.

- [ ] **R3 — Change Contract must be reliable and artifact-backed.**
  - **Behavior**: The Change Contract workflow must:
    - upload `contract_report.json` as an artifact every run
    - avoid failing due to “in-flight main checks” (it may retry/poll as it already does)
    - fail only when the merged change contract is conclusively unmet
  - **Files**:
    - `.github/workflows/change-contract.yml` — preserve/strengthen the “defer when main checks still in flight” behavior and artifact upload.
    - `api/scripts/validate_merged_change_contract.py` (if needed) — stable JSON schema and reason codes (only if the spec requires changes here; otherwise keep out-of-scope).

- [ ] **R4 — CI output must be actionable and minimal.**
  - **Behavior**: When a gate fails, the logs must include:
    - which gate failed (commit evidence, spec quality, runtime drift, tests, web build)
    - the exact local remediation command (for example: `python3 scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py --base origin/main --head HEAD --require-changed-evidence`)
  - **Files**:
    - `.github/workflows/thread-gates.yml`
    - `scripts/worktree_pr_guard.py`

## API Changes

- **None required** for this spec. CI pipeline changes live in GitHub workflows + local guard scripts.
- If the implementation needs a public API surface for CI state, it must be proposed as a separate spec (out of scope here) so we don’t mix operational plumbing with runtime product API.

## Data Model

### Commit evidence record (file-backed, validated)

Commit-evidence is represented as JSON files in `docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_*.json` and validated by `scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py`.

Minimum schema expectations (already enforced by validator; listed here as contract):

```yaml
CommitEvidence:
  date: string               # ISO-ish date
  thread_branch: string
  commit_scope: string
  files_owned: [string]
  local_validation: { status: "pass"|"fail"|"pending", ... }
  ci_validation: { status: "pass"|"fail"|"pending", ... }
  deploy_validation: { status: "pass"|"fail"|"pending", ... }
  phase_gate: { can_move_next_phase: boolean, ... }
  idea_ids: [string]         # must include "ci-pipeline" when this spec is used
  spec_ids: [string]         # must include "ci-pipeline" when this spec is used
  task_ids: [string]
  contributors: [object]
  agent: { name: string, version: string }
  evidence_refs: [string]
  change_files: [string]     # must cover all changed (non-evidence) paths for diff range
  change_intent: "runtime_feature"|"runtime_fix"|"process_only"|"docs_only"|"test_only"
  e2e_validation?: object    # required when change_intent is runtime_feature/runtime_fix
```

## Verification Scenarios

Run these from repo root unless otherwise noted.

1) **Commit evidence validates for a single evidence file**

```bash
python3 scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py --file docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_2026-04-25_spec_task_card_contract.json
```

**Expected output** (substring match):
- `OK: evidence validation passed for docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_2026-04-25_spec_task_card_contract.json`

2) **Commit evidence validates across a diff range (PR-style)**

```bash
git fetch origin main
python3 scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py --base origin/main --head HEAD --require-changed-evidence
```

**Expected output**:
- exits `0`
- prints at least one `OK: evidence validation passed for ...`

3) **Local thread gate predicts push readiness**

```bash
gi
```

## cli-binary-name-conflict

- **Spec file**: `specs/cli-binary-name-conflict.md`
- **Canonical attempt**: branch `task/task_fe488f9d0ef`, tip `4df5ab5a`, 2026-03-31

### Content

```markdown
# Spec: CLI Binary Name Conflict — Rename `cc` to `coh` (or add alias)

## Purpose

`npm i -g coherence-cli` installs a binary named `cc`, which shadows `/usr/bin/cc` (Apple's
clang compiler wrapper) on macOS. Every developer or agent that runs `cc` in a shell that
has the npm global bin directory first in `PATH` gets the Coherence CLI instead of the
compiler. This is the first friction point encountered during onboarding and blocks macOS
contributors from using both tools.

The fix: rename the primary binary to `coh` (short, memorable, no system conflicts) while
keeping `cc` as a deprecated alias for backward compatibility during the transition period.

---

## Current State

`cli/package.json`:
```json
"bin": {
  "cc": "./bin/cc.mjs"
}
```

`cli/bin/cc.mjs` — entry point shebang: `#!/usr/bin/env node`

The binary `cc` conflicts with:
- `/usr/bin/cc` — Apple clang on macOS
- `cc` — C compiler wrapper on Linux (gcc symlink)
- Any shell alias `cc` that contributors may have set

---

## Required Changes

### 1. `cli/package.json`
```json
"bin": {
  "coh": "./bin/cc.mjs",
  "cc":  "./bin/cc.mjs"
}
```

Add both entries so existing `cc` usage keeps working. Deprecate `cc` in a future major version.

### 2. `cli/bin/cc.mjs` — deprecation warning for `cc` invocation
```js
const invokedAs = path.basename(process.argv[1]);
if (invokedAs === 'cc') {
  process.stderr.write(
    '\x1b[33m[coherence-cli] Warning: `cc` shadows the system C compiler.\n' +
    'Run `npm i -g coherence-cli` again after this release to get `coh`.\n\x1b[0m'
  );
}
```

### 3. Documentation updates
- `README.md`: Replace all `cc <command>` examples with `coh <command>`
- `CLAUDE.md`: Add note about `coh` as primary binary
- `cli/bin/cc.mjs` help text: update header from `cc — Coherence Network CLI` to `coh — Coherence Network CLI`

### 4. npm publish
After the `package.json` change, bump version to next minor (`x.y+1.0`) and publish:
```
cd cli && npm version minor && npm publish
```

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `cli/package.json` | Add `"coh"` bin entry alongside `"cc"` |
| `cli/bin/cc.mjs` | Add deprecation warning when invoked as `cc`; update help header |
| `README.md` | Replace `cc` examples with `coh` |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Note `coh` as primary binary name |

---

## Verification Scenarios

### Scenario 1: `coh` resolves after install
- **Setup**: `npm i -g coherence-cli` (new version)
- **Action**: Run `coh help`
- **Expected**: CLI help output, no error
- **Edge**: `which coh` returns npm global bin path

### Scenario 2: `cc` still works (backward compat)
- **Setup**: Same install
- **Action**: Run `cc help`
- **Expected**: CLI help output + deprecation warning on stderr
- **Edge**: `cc ideas` continues to work for existing scripts

### Scenario 3: No conflict with system compiler
- **Setup**: macOS with Xcode command line tools; npm global bin in PATH
- **Action**: Run `cc --version`
- **Expected**: Coherence CLI deprecation warning + help (not clang)
- **But**: `$(which cc) --version` resolves to `/usr/bin/cc` = clang (PATH order independent)
- **Fix validated**: `coh --version` is unambiguous

### Scenario 4: CLAUDE.md reflects new name
- **Action**: `grep -i 'coh\|cc ' CLAUDE.md | head -5`
- **Expected**: Documentation references `coh` as the primary CLI binary

### Scenario 5: npm package version bumped
- **Action**: `npm show coherence-cli version`
- **Expected**: Version number higher than previous; both `cc` and `coh` listed in `bin`

---

## Risks and Assumptions

- **Existing scripts**: Any CI or runner script that calls `cc` will continue working due to the
  alias — no breaking change.
- **npm publish access**: Requires npm token with publish rights to the `coherence-cli` package.
- **PATH order**: The deprecation warning only fires when the npm bin directory is before
  `/usr/bin` in PATH — which is the problematic configuration to warn about.
- **Future breaking change**: Removing `cc` from `bin` is a breaking change; defer to a major
  version bump.

---

## Known Gaps and Follow-up Tasks

- **Shell completion**: If `cc` was registered for tab completion, `coh` needs its own
  registration (`coh completion >> ~/.zshrc`).
- **Docker images**: Agent containers that install `coherence-cli` globally may need updating
  once `cc` is removed.
- **`cc` alias removal**: Track in a follow-up idea; remove in next major version after 3-month
  deprecation window.
- **Binary verification test**: Add a CI step that asserts `which coh` succeeds after
  `npm install`.
```

---

  - "No scope creep: prefer reusing existing endpoints (`/api/meta/*`, registry discovery) over inventing new ones unless required for proof."
  - "Adapters over features: CLI and MCP stay thin; business logic remains in API services."

# Spec: CLI & MCP Surface (Umbrella)

## Summary

The Coherence Network has two primary automation surfaces: the human CLI (`coherence-cli`, invoked as `coh`) and the agent-facing MCP server (`coherence-mcp-server`). This spec defines a **single proof-oriented “surface report”** that makes it obvious whether the surfaces are working, aligned, and improving over time: what commands/tools exist, what API endpoints they map to, what is missing, and what the system’s traceability coverage looks like.

This is an umbrella spec for the `cli-mcp-surface` super-idea. It does **not** replace deeper child specs; it defines the **integration proof surface** and the minimum contracts that keep the CLI + MCP surfaces coherent as the network grows.

## Purpose

- Make “is it working?” legible in one command and one API-backed report.
- Reduce drift between CLI commands, MCP tools, and API endpoints.
- Create a ratcheting proof mechanism: each release can strengthen thresholds (coverage, parity) without breaking automation.

## Requirements

### R1 — Surface Report (single canonical proof artifact)

- **Requirement**: Add a canonical “surface report” that answers:
  - **CLI**: what top-level commands exist (and which are non-interactive + JSON-capable)
  - **MCP**: how many tools exist, which are read-only vs write, and their names
  - **API**: meta coverage summary (endpoints, traced, coverage_pct) and key discovery endpoints needed for surfacing the system
  - **Parity**: what is aligned vs missing (explicitly list gaps)
- **Files expected to change**
  - `cli/bin/coh.mjs` — add a new top-level command entry (e.g. `surface`) and wire args
  - `cli/lib/commands/meta.mjs` (or new `cli/lib/commands/surface.mjs`) — implement surface report output (text + JSON)
  - `mcp-server/coherence_mcp_server/server.py` — expose tool inventory in a machine-auditable way (see R3)
  - `api/app/services/meta_service.py` and `api/app/routers/meta.py` — only if existing meta endpoints are insufficient for the report (prefer reuse)

### R2 — Automation-friendly JSON output (proof clarity)

- **Requirement**: The CLI surface report must support a non-interactive mode that prints **valid JSON** to stdout (no ANSI), suitable for logs and CI.
- **Files expected to change**
  - `cli/lib/ui/json.mjs` — reuse existing JSON printing helpers (no duplicate JSON formatting)
  - `cli/bin/coh.mjs` — ensure global flags allow `--json` style output for the report (or consistent existing pattern)
  - `cli/lib/config.mjs` — only if output-format preference already exists and can be reused

### R3 — MCP tool inventory is stable + auditable

- **Requirement**: The MCP server must have a stable way to audit tool inventory locally:
  - tool count
  - tool names
  - for each tool: description + input schema (at minimum)
  - optional: a version string for the inventory
- **Files expected to change**
  - `mcp-server/coherence_mcp_server/server.py` — ensure `TOOLS` can be enumerated and safely JSON-serialized for audit commands/tests
  - `api/app/services/mcp_tool_registry.py` — if registry is authoritative for tool mapping, ensure it can provide a structured inventory to the MCP layer
  - `mcp-server/index.mjs` — optional: support a `--version` / `--print-tools` mode without starting stdio server (only if needed for audit)

### R4 — API self-discovery backing (no source scraping)

- **Requirement**: The surface report should be backed by API self-discovery endpoints rather than scraping local source. The minimum set:
  - `GET /api/meta/summary` (or equivalent)
  - `GET /api/meta/endpoints`
  - `GET /api/meta/modules`
- **Files expected to change**
  - `api/app/routers/meta.py` — ensure endpoints exist and are stable
  - `api/app/services/meta_service.py` — ensure summary/coverage fields needed by the report exist
  - `cli/lib/commands/meta.mjs` — reuse these endpoints and avoid new bespoke endpoints where possible

### R5 — Proof ratchet: “working yet” and “clearer over time”

- **Requirement**: Define a small set of **ratcheting thresholds** in the report so proof becomes clearer:
  - `meta.coverage_pct` should not regress (ratchet threshold stored in config or asserted in CI later; this spec only defines fields)
  - MCP tool inventory size should not regress without an explicit breaking-change note
  - CLI surface report should include a `generated_at` timestamp and a `surface_version`
- **Files expected to change**
  - `cli/lib/commands/*` (surface report output) — include ratchet fields and counts
  - `mcp-server/coherence_mcp_server/server.py` — include tool inventory versioning fields if implemented

## API changes

This umbrella spec **prefers zero new API endpoints**. It relies on existing (or already-spec’d) meta endpoints (see `specs/meta-self-discovery.md`) and existing discovery endpoints where helpful (e.g. registry discovery).

If any new API endpoint is required, it must be:
- read-only
- derived from existing runtime registries (FastAPI router registry, MCP registry)
- explicitly justified as necessary for proof (not convenience)

## Data model

Define a JSON shape for the CLI surface report output. This is a **projection** (water/gas) derived from runtime surfaces; it is not a new persisted database schema.

```json
{
  "surface_version": "1",
  "generated_at": "2026-04-25T00:00:00Z",
  "cli": {
    "binary": "coh",
    "commands_top_level": ["ideas","specs","meta","agent","nodes","skills","surface"],
    "json_capable": ["meta","surface"]
  },
  "mcp": {
    "server": "coherence-mcp-server",
    "tool_count": 74,
    "tools_sample": ["coherence_list_ideas","coherence_get_idea"]
  },
  "api": {
    "meta": {
      "endpoint_count": 0,
      "traced_count": 0,
      "spec_coverage_pct": 0.0
    }
  },
  "gaps": [
    {"kind":"parity","surface":"cli","missing":"surface report json"},
    {"kind":"cove
```

# Spec: Non-Interactive Identity for Agents (CLI)

## Purpose

Automation and AI agents need a deterministic way to set **which** contributor the Coherence CLI represents without TTY prompts or surprising auto-generated ids. The CLI already has `coh identity set <id>` and non-TTY auto-detect, but it does not read a standard environment variable, so operators cannot set identity for a single process the same way as `COHERENCE_API_KEY`. This spec requires env-based resolution, visible proof via `getContributorSource()`, and clear documentation that improves over time through a stable, grep-friendly `coh identity` output.

## Summary

**Problem:** `coh identity setup` is TTY-oriented; `getContributorId()` only reads `~/.coherence-network/config.json`, not `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID`.

**Goal:** `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID` + correct precedence, accurate `getContributorSource()`, and proof in `showIdentity` (including optional JSON for agents).

**Parent:** [Agent CLI](../ideas/agent-cli.md) — this spec is a sub-scope under the CLI idea.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Env var:** Implement `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID`. Validate with the same `CONTRIBUTOR_ID_PATTERN` as `setIdentity()`. **Precedence:** when set (non-empty after trim), **env wins** over `config.json` for `getContributorId()` in that process, so CI does not have to write config files. Document the precedence next to the existing `COHERENCE_API_*` table in `cli/lib/config.mjs` comments and `cli/README.md`.
- [ ] **R2 — Source labels:** `getContributorSource()` returns a stable, human- and machine-parseable string identifying whether the id came from env vs `config.json` (e.g. includes the substring `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID` for the env case, or a documented `env:` label).
- [ ] **R3 — Proof in `coh identity`:** `showIdentity` must print resolved `contributor_id` and the winning source. Add **--json** on this subcommand (if not already) with keys at least: `contributor_id`, `source`, `valid` (boolean) so shell scripts can `grep` / `jq` without TTY.
- [ ] **R4 — set command:** `coh identity set <contributor_id>` remains: persists to `config.json` only, exit `0` on success, non-zero and stderr on invalid id (no prompts).
- [ ] **R5 — ensureIdentity:** If `getContributorId()` is non-null after R1, `ensureIdentity()` must return that id without running the non-TTY auto-detect / host registration path; regression-test the “no file, no env” non-TTY case.

**Clarify invalid env (pick one, document in README):** if `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID` is set but invalid, **prefer fail fast** (stderr + non-zero) over silent fallback — avoids wrong-identity security foot-guns; if impl chooses ignore-and-fallback, it must be explicit in docs.

**Out-of-R6 follow-up (optional):** one-line “id @ source” in `coh status` — not required in this spec; add under Out of Scope if not done here.

## API

No new HTTP routes. Optional client-side `GET /api/identity/{id}` after set — not required.

## Data model

- `~/.coherence-network/config.json` — `contributor_id` (unchanged).
- Process environment: `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID` (new, string).
- No database or graph schema changes.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `cli/lib/config.mjs` — add `readEnv` path for `COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID` in `getContributorId` / `getContributorSource` with documented precedence; align with existing env pattern at top of file.
- `cli/lib/identity.mjs` — confirm `ensureIdentity` does not side-step env id (early return on parsed id).
- `cli/lib/commands/identity.mjs` — `showIdentity` (and `setIdentity` help text if needed); add `--json` to identity subcommand output when requested globally by CLI pattern.
- `cli/README.md` — Environment variables: identity, CI examples, precedence table.
- (Optional) `api/tests/` or `cli/` smoke — only if a minimal automated test is added; not mandatory for the spec to be implementable; **Acceptance** below uses **manual validation**.

## Acceptance Tests

- **Manual validation** (CI or local): run all commands in the `Verification` section; all expected exit codes and substrings pass. This is the **primary** contract until a dedicated `cli` test file exists.
- Optional: add a **future** `api/tests/test_cli_identity_precedence.py` (or `cli` vitest) only if the repo’s impl agent can wire subprocess checks without scope creep; not a blocker for v1.

## Verification

```bash
# 1) Env-only identity (no file write) — from repo root, with fresh HOME or no contributor_id in config
export COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID="agent-ci-smoke-01"
cd cli && node bin/coh.mjs identity
# Expect: exit 0, stdout or stderr (per UX) includes agent-ci-smoke-01; source line references env / COHERENCE_CONTRIBUTOR_ID
```

```bash
# 2) Persisted id — unset env, then
cd cli && node bin/coh.mjs identity set "my-bot-contributor" && node bin/coh.mjs identity
# Expect: exit 0, source shows config.json (or project label) and id my-bot-contributor
```

```bash
# 3) JSON (after impl)
cd cli && node bin/coh.mjs identity --json 2>/dev/null
# Expect: valid JSON (if flag supported) with contributor_id and source fields; exit 0 when identity valid
```

```bash
# 4) Regression — no env, no config, non-TTY: pipe or script must still trigger auto-detect (existing "Registered as:"-style path)
```

```bash
# 5) 
```

# Spec: Coherence Algorithm & Resonance Engine

## Purpose

The coherence algorithm engine is the computational heart of the platform. It translates the abstract claim "this idea is coherent and resonating" into measured, observable numbers that grow with real activity. Without this engine, coherence scores are static stubs, resonance is keyword matching, and the platform cannot show whether it is working.

This spec closes four gaps in the existing algorithm layer: triadic scoring (idea × contributor × concept), the breath lifecycle state machine, semantic context enrichment for resonance pairs, and collective coherence health across the full idea graph. Together they provide the proof layer — living evidence that the platform's core thesis holds.

## Requirements

### R1: Triadic Coherence Score

A triadic score measures how well an idea sits at the intersection of its contributor community and the concept layer. The triangle has three vertices:

- **Idea vertex** (`idea_score`): CRK coherence of the idea's text symbol against its top-10 concept matches. Computed via `concept_resonance_kernel.compare_concepts()` averaged across matched concept symbols.
- **Contributor vertex** (`contributor_score`): Mean CRK coherence across active contributors' belief/interest profiles against the idea. Falls back to 0.5 when contributor profiles are absent.
- **Concept vertex** (`concept_score`): Fraction of the idea's top-10 concept matches that have concept files in the DB with at least one cross-reference edge.

Final `triadic_score` = harmonic mean of the three vertex scores.

New file: `api/app/services/triadic_scoring_service.py`
New route: `GET /api/algorithm/triadic/{idea_id}` in `api/app/routers/algorithm.py`

### R2: Breath Lifecycle State Machine

Every idea moves through a lifecycle: compose → expand → validate → contract → release → rest. These map to the ice/water/gas phase states from the knowledge-and-resonance parent. The state machine enforces allowed transitions and persists the current phase.

Allowed transitions:
```
compose → expand
expand → validate
expand → contract
validate → contract
validate → release
contract → compose
contract → rest
release → rest
rest → compose
```

- Current phase stored on the idea record (new `breath_phase` string column, nullable, default `compose`)
- `GET /api/algorithm/breath/{idea_id}` returns `{idea_id, phase, transitioned_at, allowed_next}`
- `POST /api/algorithm/breath/{idea_id}` with `{phase: "expand"}` transitions the idea; returns 422 if the transition is not in the allowed graph

New file: `api/app/services/breath_lifecycle_service.py`
Add to: `api/app/routers/algorithm.py`

### R3: Semantic Context for Resonance Pairs

The existing CRK resonance endpoint (`GET /api/resonance/ideas/{idea_id}`) returns pairs with `crk_score`, `ot_distance`, `coherence`. It does not explain *why* two ideas resonate — which concepts they share, which Living Codex axes align.

Enrich each `ResonancePair` with `semantic_context`:
- `matched_concepts`: list of concept IDs (from `docs/vision-kb/concepts/`) that appear in both idea texts
- `shared_axes`: list of axis names from the 53 Living Codex axes that apply to both ideas
- `belief_alignment`: 0.0–1.0 cosine similarity of the two ideas' concept vectors

The enrichment is a post-processing pass on existing `ResonancePair` results — it does not change CRK scores, only annotates.

New file: `api/app/services/semantic_context_service.py`
Modify: `api/app/services/idea_resonance_service.py` → `find_resonances_for_idea()` to call `enrich_resonance_with_context()`
Modify: `api/app/routers/resonance.py` → `ResonancePairOut` model adds `semantic_context: dict | None`

### R4: Collective Coherence Health

The platform needs a single aggregate view of how well the entire idea graph is resonating, flowing, and where friction is high.

`GET /api/algorithm/collective-health` returns:
- `score` (0.0–1.0): weighted mean of `coherence_signal_depth_service.compute_coherence_score()["score"]` and the mean triadic score across all ideas
- `flow` (0.0–1.0): fraction of ideas cu
```

# Spec: coherence-cli npm Package — Zero-Dep, 15 Commands, Published

## Purpose

`coherence-cli` is the human and agent terminal interface to Coherence Network. It is already published to npm (v0.12.1, 24 versions) but carries 5 runtime dependencies (boxen, chalk, cli-table3, inquirer, ora) that inflate install size, introduce supply-chain risk, and contradict the zero-dependency design intent. This spec locks the package to zero production dependencies, defines the 15 canonical core commands, ships a GitHub Actions publish pipeline, and adds a proof metric (`coh status --npm`) so it is always clear whether the package is healthy and being installed by real users.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Zero production dependencies**: Remove `boxen`, `chalk`, `cli-table3`, `inquirer`, `ora` from `dependencies`. Replace each with a thin native wrapper:
  - `cli/lib/ui/table.mjs` — renders ASCII tables via string formatting (no cli-table3)
  - `cli/lib/ui/spinner.mjs` — writes `\r` + ANSI erase + frame to `process.stdout` (no ora)
  - `cli/lib/ui/box.mjs` — draws a bordered box with Unicode box-drawing chars (no boxen)
  - `cli/lib/ui/prompt.mjs` — uses `readline/promises` for interactive input (no inquirer)
  - ANSI color: replace all `chalk.*()` calls with template-literal helpers in `cli/lib/ui/ansi.mjs` (e.g. `c.green(s)` = `\x1b[32m${s}\x1b[0m`).

- [ ] **R2 — 15 core commands**: The following 15 commands form the canonical surface. All must be listed by `coh help` with a one-line description:

  | # | Command | Description |
  |---|---------|-------------|
  | 1 | `ideas` | List top ideas with coherence scores |
  | 2 | `idea <slug>` | Show idea detail, specs, and absorbed ideas |
  | 3 | `specs` | List specs for an idea |
  | 4 | `spec <slug>` | Show spec frontmatter + requirements |
  | 5 | `status` | API health + optional `--npm` download count |
  | 6 | `identity` | Show current identity (subcommand: `setup`) |
  | 7 | `stake <idea> <cc>` | Stake CC on an idea |
  | 8 | `fork <idea>` | Fork an idea into a child |
  | 9 | `share` | Submit a new idea |
  | 10 | `contribute` | Record a contribution |
  | 11 | `resonance` | Live activity feed (recent contributions + ideas) |
  | 12 | `nodes` | Federation node status |
  | 13 | `msg <to> <text>` | Send a message to a node or contributor |
  | 14 | `inbox` | Read incoming messages |
  | 15 | `config` | View/set local CLI config (api url, identity path) |

- [ ] **R3 — npm publish pipeline**: `.github/workflows/npm-publish.yml` runs `npm publish --access public` from `cli/` when a tag matching `v*` is pushed to `main`. Uses `NPM_TOKEN` repository secret. Includes `npm test` as a pre-publish gate.

- [ ] **R4 — Identity-first onboarding**: `coh identity setup` uses `readline/promises` to collect: display name, handle (@ prefix), identity provider choice (wallet / GitHub / email / anonymous). Writes config to `~/.coherence-network/config.json`. Prints confirmation box using native `box.mjs`.

- [ ] **R5 — Proof metric**: `coh status --npm` fetches `https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/point/last-week/coherence-cli` and prints:
  ```
  npm downloads (last 7 days): 142
  ```
  Exit code 0 if count > 0, exit code 1 if 0 or API unreachable (signals dead package).

- [ ] **R6 — API URL override**: All HTTP calls read base URL from `COHERENCE_API_URL` env var, falling back to `https://api.coherencycoin.com`. `coh config set api-url <url>` persists the override to `~/.coherence-network/config.json`.

## Research Inputs

- `2026-04-26` — npm registry — coherence-cli@0.12.1 published, 24 versions, 5 deps, dist 489 kB uncompressed. Zero-dep target reduces install to ~50 kB.
- `2026-04-26` — specs/coherence-cli-comprehensive.md (done) — defines the 15-command surface and zero-dep constraint; this spec operationalizes publish pipeline and proof metrics.

## API Contract

### `GET https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/point/last-week/coherence-cli`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "downloads": 142,
  "start": "2026-04-19",
  "end": "2026-04-25",
  "package": "coherence-cli"
}
```

### `GET /api/health` (Coherence API)

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "0.13.0"
}
```

## Data Model

```yaml
LocalConfig (~/.coherence-network/config.json):
  api_url: string           # default https://api.coherencycoin.com
  identity_path: string     # default ~/.coherence-network/identity.json
  handle: string            # @-prefixed user handle

LocalIdentity (~/.coherence-network/identity.json):
  handle: string
  display_name: string
  provider: string          # wallet | github | email | anonymous
  created_at: string        # ISO 8601
```

## Files to Create/Modify

- `cli/package.json` — remove boxen, chalk, cli-table3, inquirer, ora from `dependencies`; move to `devDependencies` during transition if needed, then remove entirely
- `cli/
```

# Spec: Community project ↔ small funder match

## Summary

Local and intentional-community projects need a **fast, honest on-ramp** for small funders: a one-screen explanation of **goal**, **budget** (in CC terms and plain language), and **early proof** so the right backer can decide whether a first conversation is worth scheduling. The platform already exposes funder-oriented summaries via `GET /api/ideas/showcase` with `IdeaShowcaseItem` (clear ask, `IdeaShowcaseBudget`, `early_proof`). This spec adds a **dedicated match surface**: **readiness tiers**, the **smallest proof bar** expected before a first meeting, **explainable** project–funder fit (thesis/sector/tags), and **lightweight interest capture** to measure whether the system is working—without building payments, KYC, or full marketplace mechanics in v1.

**Parent context**: Absorbed under [external-presence](ideas/external-presence.md) as part of ecosystem marketplace; implementation should stay aligned with [creator-economy-promotion](creator-economy-promotion.md)-style “proof you can share” patterns where applicable.

## Purpose

- **Projects** can see what funders need at each tier and improve their “ask” until they cross the bar.
- **Funders** (small tickets, place-based, mission-aligned) scan a **short list** and open a **single brief** that answers: what, how much, what’s already true, and why this might fit their thesis.
- **The network** can answer: *Is matching happening?* via interest events and optional dashboard fields consistent with [docs/MVP-DASHBOARD-METRICS.md](docs/MVP-DASHBOARD-METRICS.md).

## Open questions (addressed in this spec)

1. **Smallest proof before a first meeting?**  
   Treated as a **readiness function**, not a single global answer. Define **three tiers** (e.g. `listed` / `conversation_ready` / `due_diligence_ready`). Each tier has a **smallest proof checklist** (e.g. one-line goal + budget range, named steward + public link, third-party reference or verifiable artifact). The API returns `readiness_tier` and `smallest_proof_for_meeting` for the **next** tier so projects know what to add. Defaults live in `api/config/` (or a small JSON file keyed by spec id) so ops can tune without code changes.

2. **Which first three projects are ready to share a clear ask this month?**  
   v1: **`funder_match` config** lists up to **three** `idea_id` values (curated), validated at startup. The public page **highlights** those; `GET /api/funder-match/ready` may union curated IDs with any other ideas that pass the automated readiness threshold. Admin-only override endpoint is **out of scope** unless an existing admin pattern exists—prefer file/config rotation for v1.

3. **How do we improve, show whether it’s working, and clarify proof over time?**  
   - **Improve**: Expose `match_quality_notes` (string[]) on the brief—human-readable, deterministic reasons (e.g. “Thesis tag overlap: water, commons”).  
   - **Working**: `POST /api/funder-match/interest` records an event (project_id, optional funder_pseudonym_id, `idempotency_key`, `source=web|api`, timestamp). Counts feed portfolio metrics.  
   - **Clearer proof**: Version the **proof block** in the API (integer `proof_schema_version`); when showcase logic changes, bump version so clients can compare cohorts.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 (Readiness list)**: `GET /api/funder-match/ready` — Query params: `limit` (default 12, max 50), `offset`, optional `min_tier` (`listed|conversation_ready|due_diligence_ready`), optional `thesis` (comma-separated tags; filter projects with overlapping tags). Response: `items[]` with `project_id` (idea id), `title`, `one_line_goal`, `readiness_tier`, `budget` (same numeric shape as `IdeaShowcaseBudget` or a shared embedded model), `early_proof` (string), `smallest_proof_for_meeting` (string, **actionable** next step to reach the next tier), `thesis_tags` (string[], from idea metadata or dedicated field), `curated_spotlight` (bool — true for one of the three curated IDs). **HTTP 200**; empty list allowed.

- [ ] **R2 (Single brief)**: `GET /api/funder-match/brief` — Query: `project_id` (required), optional `funder_thesis` (string, free text or tag list; used only for `match_explanation`). Response: `CommunityProjectProfile`-shaped object: `project_id`, `title`, `narrative_goal` (from idea description, trimmed), `budget` object, `early_proof`, `readiness_tier`, `open_questions_unresolved` (count and optional top 2), `smallest_proof_for_meeting`, `match_explanation` (structured: `{ "signals": [ {"name": "thesis_overlap", "detail": "…"} ] }` ), `links` (e.g. `/ideas/{id}`, public demo URL if present in metadata). **404** if `project_id` unknown. **400** for malformed `project_id`. If project **below** `conversation_ready` for anonymous public brief, return **404** with `detail` code `not_ready` (body includes `readiness_tier` for transparency) — funders should not see half-baked pages.

- [ ] **R3 (Inter
```

> **Parent idea**: [knowledge-and-resonance](../ideas/knowledge-and-resonance.md)

# Spec: Concept-as-Idea Registration

## Purpose

The Living Collective KB holds 51 concepts — the vocabulary and living structures of the community. Currently these concepts exist only in the graph DB and KB markdown files. They are not visible in the idea pipeline and carry no lifecycle stage, no coherence score, and no tracked progress.

This spec auto-registers each KB concept as a tracked idea. Once registered, a concept appears in the portfolio dashboard alongside every other idea: it has a stage (mirroring its KB status), a coherence score (derived from content richness and cross-reference density), and full pipeline visibility. The system gains a closed loop: enriching a concept in the KB raises its coherence score, which surfaces in the portfolio.

## Requirements

### R1 — Status-to-Stage Mapping

KB concepts carry a `status` frontmatter field. Map it to `IdeaStage` when registering:

| KB status | `IdeaStage` |
|-----------|-------------|
| `seed`    | `none` |
| `expanding` | `specced` |
| `deepening` | `implementing` |
| `complete` | `complete` |

No status / unknown → `none`.

### R2 — Coherence Score Derivation

Each concept-idea gets a coherence score (0.0–1.0) computed at sync time:

```
status_depth  = {seed: 0.10, expanding: 0.40, deepening: 0.70, complete: 1.00}
cross_ref_density = min(cross_ref_count / 10.0, 1.0)   # 10+ refs → 1.0
story_density = min(story_token_count / 2000.0, 1.0)   # 2000+ tokens → 1.0

coherence_score = 0.5 * status_depth + 0.3 * cross_ref_density + 0.2 * story_density
```

Cross-ref count is the number of `→ lc-*` references in the concept file. Story token count is `len(story_text) / 4` (character-based approximation).

### R3 — Idea Identity and Tags

Each concept-idea is registered with:
- `idea_id` = `"lc-{concept_id}"` (e.g. `lc-pulse`, `lc-space`)
- `name` = concept name from KB frontmatter
- `description` = first paragraph of concept story (≤ 300 chars)
- `pillar` = `"foundation"`
- `tags` = `["living-collective", "concept", "lc-id:{concept_id}"]`
- `workspace_id` = `"coherence-network"`
- `potential_value` = coherence_score (treated as a 0–1 value proxy)
- `estimated_cost` = `0.1` (concepts self-realize through tending, not external cost)
- `confidence` = `0.8`

### R4 — Idempotent Sync

Running the sync multiple times must never duplicate ideas:
- On first run: create missing ideas (`created` count increases)
- On subsequent runs: update `stage`, `coherence_score` (via score field), and `tags` on existing ideas (`updated` count increases)
- Detection: look up by `idea_id = "lc-{concept_id}"` — if exists, PATCH; if not, POST

### R5 — API Endpoint

`POST /api/ideas/sync-concepts` — authenticated (API key required).

Response:
```json
{
  "created": 12,
  "updated": 39,
  "errors": [],
  "total": 51
}
```

On partial failure: include errors list with `{concept_id, error}` entries; HTTP 200 with error details (not 500).

### R6 — Standalone Script

`scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py` must work standalone:

```bash
python scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py                         # all 51 concepts, local API
python scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py --dry-run               # show what would change
python scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py lc-pulse lc-space       # sync specific concepts
python scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py --api-url https://... --api-key prod-key
```

## API Contract

### `POST /api/ideas/sync-concepts`

Triggers full sync of all 51 core LC concepts as tracked ideas.

**Request headers**: `X-API-Key: {key}`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "created": 12,
  "updated": 39,
  "errors": [],
  "total": 51
}
```

**Response 200 (partial failure)**
```json
{
  "created": 10,
  "updated": 38,
  "errors": [
    {"concept_id": "lc-shared-hold", "error": "concept file not found"}
  ],
  "total": 51
}
```

### `GET /api/ideas?tags=living-collective`

Returns all concept-ideas. Existing endpoint — no changes needed. Verify 51 results after sync.

### `GET /api/ideas/{lc-pulse}`

Returns single concept-idea by its `idea_id`. Verify `stage` and `coherence_score` fields are present.

## Data Model

No new DB columns required. Concept-ideas use the existing `Idea` model. Concept identity is encoded in `tags`:

```yaml
Idea:
  id: "lc-pulse"                          # concept idea_id
  name: "The Pulse"
  description: "432 Hz — the cell and the field thrive as one movement"
  pillar: "foundation"
  stage: "complete"                        # mapped from KB status
  coherence_score: 0.82                    # computed at sync time
  tags:
    - "living-collective"
    - "concept"
    - "lc-id:lc-pulse"                     # reverse-lookup tag
  potential_value: 0.82                    # mirrors coherence_score
  estimated_cost: 0.1
  confidence: 0.8
```

## Files

### New files

- `scripts/sync_concepts_as_ideas.py` — standalone script; reads `docs/vision-kb/concepts/*.md`, calls API to register/update each concept as an idea
- `api/app/services/concept_idea_bridge_service.py` — service with:
  - `map_kb_status_to_stage(status: str) -> IdeaStage`
  - `compute_coherence_score(parsed_concept: dict) -> float`
  - `ensure_concept_idea(concept_id: str, parsed: dict, api_url: str, api_key: str) -> str` → `"created"` | `"updated"` | `"error"`
  - `sync_all_concepts_as_ideas(api_url: str, api_key: str, dry_run: bool) -> dict`
- `api/tests/test_flow_concept_registration.py` — flow tests (see Acceptance Tests)

### Modified files

- `api/app/routers/ideas.py` — add `POST /api/ideas/sync-concepts` endpoint calling `concept_idea_bridge_service.sync_all_concepts_as_ideas()`
- `scripts/sync_kb_to_db.py` — add optional `--register-as-idea` flag that calls `sync_concepts_as_ideas.py` after syncing; no changes to core sync logic

## Acceptance
```

  - "DB schema must handle both SQLite (dev) and PostgreSQL (prod) — use unified_db.py"
  - "JSON file api/config/news-sources.json is seeded into DB on startup if table is empty; file is not removed"
  - "fetch_feeds only fetches type=rss and type=atom via XML parsing; type=json uses JSONFeed v1.1 HTTP response body"
  - "type=api sources remain in config/DB but are not fetched by fetch_feeds (they require external integration)"
  - "Health fields are updated in-process after each fetch attempt, not via a background scheduler"
  - "Source CRUD endpoints require X-API-Key; GET endpoints are public"
---

# Spec: Configurable News Sources

## Purpose

News sources power the discovery feed — matching world signals to ideas in flight. When sources are hardcoded or stored in a file nobody can see, dead feeds silently disappear from the signal stream. This spec moves source configuration into the database, exposes health telemetry, adds CLI ergonomics for operators, and lets contributors subscribe to topics so their feed reflects what they actually care about.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — DB storage**: `news_sources` table holds all source config. On first boot (empty table), the service seeds from `api/config/news-sources.json`. After seed, the JSON file is the backup, not the truth. `unified_db.py` `get_session()` pattern applies.

- [ ] **R2 — Full CRUD via existing API**: The routes `GET/POST/DELETE/PATCH /api/news/sources` already exist in `api/app/routers/news.py` and `api/app/services/news_ingestion_service.py`. Their signatures remain unchanged — the impl replaces the in-memory list + JSON file backend with DB reads/writes.

- [ ] **R3 — JSON Feed v1.1 ingestion**: When a source has `type: "json"`, `fetch_feeds` fetches the URL and parses it as a JSONFeed v1.1 document (`items[].title`, `items[].url`, `items[].content_text`, `items[].date_published`). Items land in the same `NewsItem` dataclass as RSS items.

- [ ] **R4 — Source health tracking**: After each fetch attempt per source, update three DB fields: `last_fetched_at` (ISO UTC), `last_error` (error message string or null), `consecutive_failures` (int). Reset `consecutive_failures` to 0 on success. `health_status` derives as: 0 failures → `ok`, 1–2 → `degraded`, ≥3 → `broken`.

- [ ] **R5 — Health API**: `GET /api/news/sources/{id}/health` returns health snapshot for one source. `GET /api/news/sources/health` returns summary for all sources — count by status, list of broken source IDs.

- [ ] **R6 — CLI: list sources** `cc news-sources` calls `GET /api/news/sources` and prints a table with columns: id, name, type, is_active, health_status (if present).

- [ ] **R7 — CLI: add source** `cc news add <url> <name>` calls `POST /api/news/sources` with a generated ID (slugified name), the URL, optional `--type` (default `rss`), and optional `--category`. Prints the created source id.

- [ ] **R8 — Contributor topic subscriptions**: New table `contributor_news_subscriptions (contributor_id TEXT, topics JSON)`. Routes: `GET /api/contributors/{id}/news/subscriptions` and `PUT /api/contributors/{id}/news/subscriptions` (body: `{"topics": ["science", "technology"]}`). Requires API key on PUT.

- [ ] **R9 — Topic-filtered feed**: When `GET /api/news/feed?contributor_id={id}` is called and the contributor has subscriptions, filter items to those whose source has at least one category matching a subscribed topic. When no subscriptions exist, return unfiltered (current behaviour).

## API Contract

### `GET /api/news/sources`

**Query params**: `active_only=true|false` (default `false`)

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "count": 3,
  "sources": [
    {
      "id": "nature-science",
      "name": "Nature - Scientific Discoveries",
      "type": "rss",
      "url": "https://www.nature.com/nature.rss",
      "categories": ["science", "research"],
      "is_active": true,
      "health_status": "ok",
      "last_fetched_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00+00:00",
      "consecutive_failures": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

### `GET /api/news/sources/{id}/health`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "id": "nature-science",
  "health_status": "ok",
  "last_fetched_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00+00:00",
  "last_error": null,
  "consecutive_failures": 0
}
```

**Response 404**
```json
{"detail": "Source not found: nature-science"}
```

### `GET /api/news/sources/health`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "total": 45,
  "ok": 40,
  "degraded": 3,
  "broken": 2,
  "broken_ids": ["reuters-world", "financial-times"]
}
```

### `GET /api/contributors/{id}/news/subscriptions`

**Response 200**
```json
{"contributor_id": "alice", "topics": ["science", "technology"]}
```

### `PUT /api/contributors/{id}/news/subscriptions`

**Request body**
```json
{"topics": ["science", "technology", "philosophy"]}
```

**Response 200**
```json
{"contributor_id": "alice", "topics": ["science", "technology", "philosophy"]}
```

## Data Model

```yaml
news_sources:
  id: TEXT PRIMARY KEY
  name: TEXT NOT NULL
  type: TEXT DEFAULT 'rss'        # rss | atom | json | api
  url: TEXT NOT NULL
  categories: TEXT
```

*(truncated at 8KB — fetch full content via `git fetch origin e5e72f515a8d312269e98fa6d5ffc9c56d918056 && git show e5e72f515a8d312269e98fa6d5ffc9c56d918056:specs/configurable-news-sources.md`)*

# Spec: Consolidate /automation, /usage, /remote-ops into /nodes and /pipeline

## Purpose

Four pages currently show overlapping operational data, forcing operators to navigate between
screens to understand a single picture. `/automation` duplicates provider and node stats already
on `/nodes`; `/usage` duplicates provider health already on `/automation` and `/nodes`; `/remote-ops`
shows the same task queue surfaced on `/pipeline`. Consolidating into two clear pages — `/nodes`
(everything about nodes) and `/pipeline` (everything about task execution) — reduces navigation
cost, eliminates data duplication, and gives each concept a single canonical address.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Expand /nodes with remote-ops content**: The `/nodes` page gains a "Remote Control" section
  containing everything currently in `/remote-ops`: deployment/uptime status, execute-token input, task
  dispatch controls (executor, task type, model override, force-paid, PR-thread, auto-merge, wait-public,
  direction), pick-up button, and the pending queue table. This section must be a client component
  (`use client`) embedded in the otherwise server-rendered `NodesPage`.

- [ ] **R2 — Expand /pipeline with usage content**: The `/pipeline` page gains a "Resource Usage" section
  containing: daily summary (friction signal, quality awareness, host runner by-task-type breakdown,
  top tools, top attention areas) and a paginated runtime cost table (ideas ordered by runtime cost
  estimate). Provider health data is already on `/pipeline` — do not duplicate.

- [ ] **R3 — Redirect /automation → /nodes**: Replace `web/app/automation/page.tsx` with a Next.js
  `permanentRedirect('/nodes')` (HTTP 308). Keep all other files in `web/app/automation/` intact
  for potential re-use or as supporting components imported by `/nodes`.

- [ ] **R4 — Redirect /usage → /pipeline**: Replace `web/app/usage/page.tsx` with a Next.js
  `permanentRedirect('/pipeline')` (HTTP 308). Keep `web/app/usage/data.ts`, `web/app/usage/sections/`,
  and `web/app/usage/types.ts` — the pipeline page will import from these.

- [ ] **R5 — Redirect /remote-ops → /nodes**: Replace `web/app/remote-ops/page.tsx` with a Next.js
  `permanentRedirect('/nodes')` (HTTP 308). Keep supporting files (`use-remote-ops.ts`,
  `controls-section.tsx`, `deployment-uptime-section.tsx`, `queue-pipeline-section.tsx`,
  `utils.ts`) — the nodes page will import from these.

- [ ] **R6 — Update site navigation**: In `web/components/site_header.tsx`, remove the
  `{ href: "/automation", labelKey: "nav.automation" }` entry. Verify `/nodes` and `/pipeline`
  are present in the nav array (add if missing).

- [ ] **R7 — Update page context links**: In `web/components/page_context_links.tsx`, remove the
  `/automation` and `/remote-ops` entries. Update any nav links inside usage sections that point
  to `/automation` to point to `/nodes` instead.

- [ ] **R8 — Cross-link between /nodes and /pipeline**: The "Where to go next" nav at the bottom
  of both pages must include a link to the other (nodes → pipeline, pipeline → nodes).

## Research Inputs

- `2026-04-25` — Direct source reading of all four pages and their component trees, nav files, data loaders

## API Contract

No new API endpoints. All existing API calls remain:

| Page | APIs called |
|------|------------|
| /nodes (expanded) | `/api/federation/nodes`, `/api/providers/stats`, `/api/automation/usage/readiness`, `/api/agent/tasks/pending`, deployment health endpoints from `use-remote-ops.ts` |
| /pipeline (expanded) | `/api/federation/nodes`, `/api/agent/tasks/active`, `/api/agent/tasks/activity`, `/api/providers/stats`, `/api/agent/tasks?status=pending`, `/api/agent/tasks?status=running`, usage daily summary endpoints from `web/app/usage/data.ts` |

## Data Model

No data model changes. Existing types in `web/app/remote-ops/types.ts` and `web/app/usage/types.ts`
are imported by the expanded pages, not duplicated.

## Files to Create/Modify

### Replace with redirects
- `web/app/automation/page.tsx` — replace body with `permanentRedirect('/nodes')`
- `web/app/usage/page.tsx` — replace body with `permanentRedirect('/pipeline')`
- `web/app/remote-ops/page.tsx` — replace body with `permanentRedirect('/nodes')`

### Expand existing pages
- `web/app/nodes/page.tsx` — add "Remote Control" client sub-comp
```

# Spec: Contributor discovery (directory, search, profiles, activity)

## Summary

New contributors need to see who else is active and what they’re working on. This spec adds a **Contributor Directory** (CC + recent activity), **Contributor Search** (skills/interests/name), and **Contributor Profiles** (ideas invested in + tasks completed + recent activity). It also adds **proof signals** so we can tell whether discovery is working yet, and whether it improves over time.

## Purpose

When contributors can quickly browse “who’s here” and “who’s doing what,” they orient faster, trust the system more, and find collaboration paths (skills, interests, overlapping ideas). Today we have `GET /api/contributors` and portfolio endpoints, but no search, no profile aggregation endpoint, and no consistent definition of “recent activity.”

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Enriched directory listing**: Extend the contributor listing so the directory can show, per contributor, **CC balance** and a **recent activity summary**.
  - **Acceptance criteria**:
    - Listing remains paginated and backward compatible for existing callers.
    - Enrichment is opt-in via query flags (e.g. `include_cc=true`, `include_activity=true`) so default cost stays low.
    - Directory item contains: `contributor_id`, `display_name`, `claimed`, `cc_balance` (optional), `last_active` (optional), `recent_activity` (optional summary object).
  - **Files expected to change**:
    - `api/app/routers/contributors.py` — add query flags and enriched response mode
    - `api/app/services/contributor_activity_service.py` — (new) compute `recent_activity` + `last_active`
    - `api/app/services/portfolio_service.py` — reuse for CC-related totals (no duplication)

- [ ] **R2 — Search contributors**: Add a search endpoint that filters contributors by **q** (name/handle), **skills**, and **interests**.
  - **Acceptance criteria**:
    - `q` matches against `author_display_name` and `name` (case-insensitive substring).
    - `skills` matches against contributor node properties (`skills`, `resonant_roles`) using tokenized contains.
    - `interests` matches against derived interest keywords (see R3) OR stable stored keywords if present.
    - Response is paginated (`limit`, `offset`) and deterministic under repeated calls.
  - **Files expected to change**:
    - `api/app/routers/contributors.py` — new handler `search_contributors()`
    - `api/app/services/contributor_search_service.py` — (new) search + ranking rules
    - `api/app/models/contributor_search.py` — (new) request/response models

- [ ] **R3 — Contributor profile aggregation**: Add a profile endpoint that returns a single aggregated document for a contributor.
  - **Acceptance criteria**:
    - Profile includes **identity basics** (id, display name, claimed, locale, roles, optional contact fields based on consent flags if available).
    - Profile includes **portfolio**:
      - ideas invested in (stakes) via existing portfolio endpoints/services
      - tasks completed via existing portfolio endpoints/services
      - idea contributions summary via existing portfolio endpoints/services
    - Profile includes **recent activity** (same definition as R1).
    - Implementation must not “call the public API from the API” for local operation; it should aggregate from internal services.
  - **Files expected to change**:
    - `api/app/routers/contributors.py` — add `get_contributor_profile()` route
    - `api/app/services/contributor_profile_service.py` — make `build_profile()` aggregate internally (no self-HTTP)
    - `api/app/models/contributor_profile.py` — (new) Pydantic response model
    - `api/app/services/contributor_activity_service.py` — (new) shared activity computation

- [ ] **R4 — Activity definition**: Define “recent activity” in a way that is consistent, explainable, and cheap to compute.
  - **Definition** (minimum viable, server-side):
    - `last_active`: max timestamp across any of:
      - newest portfolio task completion time (if available)
      - newest contribution timestamp (if available)
      - newest stake change timestamp (if available)
    - `recent_activity` summary fields (all optional): `tasks_completed_30d`, `cc_earned_30d`, `ideas_touched_30d`
  - **Files expected to change**:
    - `api/app/services/contributor_activity_service.py` — centralize this logic
    - `api/app/services/portfolio_service.py` — reuse existing calculations where possible

- [ ] **R5 — Proof signals (“is it working yet?”)**: Add minimal discovery telemetry so we can show proof over time.
  - **Acceptance criteria**:
    - Add an endpoint returning rolling-window counts for contributor discovery usage (directory views, searches, profile views).
    - Store events as small atoms (node/edge events or existing telemetry mechanism); no large logs in DB.
    - The metrics are sufficient to answer:
      - Are people using discovery at all?
      - Is usage increasing week-over-week?
      - Which searches are common (top tokens)?
  - **Files expected to change**:
    - `api/app/routers/contributors.py` — `GET /api/contributors/discovery/metrics`
    - `api/app/services/contributor_discovery_metrics_service.py` — (new) record + aggregate metrics
    - `web/app/(new)/contributors/page.tsx` — emit view/search/profile events (thin adapter call)

## API Changes

### `GET /api/contributors`

Add optional query flags for enrichment:
- `include_cc: bool = false`
- `include_activity: bool = false`

**Response 200 (default; backward compatible)** remains `PaginatedResponse[Contributor]`.

**Response 200 (enriched mode)**:

```json
{
  "items": [
    {
      "contributor_id": "alice",
      "display_name": "Alice
```

requirements:
  - "Contributors can send direct messages to each other — existing POST /api/messages covers this; this spec extends it"
  - "Any contributor who staked or contributed to an idea can post to its group chat via POST /api/ideas/{idea_id}/chat"
  - "Idea group chat is readable by anyone (GET /api/ideas/{idea_id}/chat returns paginated messages, newest-first)"
  - "Messages support threaded replies via optional parent_message_id field; GET /api/messages/{id}/replies returns children"

  - "Replies to a message the viewer sent trigger a notification event (kind: thread_reply)"

  - "Staker eligibility check for idea chat is best-effort: when the graph returns no edges for the idea, allow the post (new ideas have no history yet)"
  - "Notifications remain pull-only — no WebSocket or push infrastructure in this phase"
  - "parent_message_id is optional — messages without it are top-level posts"
  - "Message nodes stay in the graph store; do not add a relational table for messages"

# Spec: Contributor-to-Contributor Messaging and Idea Group Chat

## Purpose

Contributors on Coherence Network need a human communication layer distinct from the machine message bus. The node message bus handles agent-to-agent coordination; this spec delivers human-to-human direct messages, idea-scoped group chat for anyone who staked or contributed, threaded replies within any message channel, and soft notifications when someone replies to you or mentions your handle.

The Phase 3 DM scaffold (`messages.py` + `message_service.py`) already handles direct messages and workspace broadcast. This spec extends it with idea-scoped channels, threading, and notification integration — building on what circulates rather than replacing it.

## Background — What Already Exists

These endpoints and services are **live and must not be reimplemented**:

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `POST /api/messages` | Send DM (to_contributor_id) or workspace broadcast (to_workspace_id) |
| `GET /api/messages/inbox/{contributor_id}` | Paginated inbox with unread count |
| `GET /api/messages/thread/{a}/{b}` | DM thread between two contributors |
| `PATCH /api/messages/{id}/read` | Mark message read |
| `GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/messages` | Workspace-scoped message list |
| `GET /notifications` | Pull-based notification events (reactions, @mentions in comments, proposal lifts) |

The notification service (`notification_service.unseen_for()`) currently surfaces events from reactions, voices, and proposals. This spec extends it to also surface `new_dm`, `thread_reply`, and `mention` events from message nodes in the graph.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Idea group chat post**: `POST /api/ideas/{idea_id}/chat` accepts `{ from_contributor_id, body, parent_message_id? }` and creates a message node with `to_idea_id={idea_id}` and an `idea-message` edge to the idea node. Returns 201 with the full message object. Eligibility check: if the contributor has a `staked-on` or `contributed-to` edge to the idea in the graph, allow; if the graph returns no edges at all for the idea, allow (new idea, no history yet). If edges exist but none connect this contributor, return 403 with `{ "detail": "must stake or contribute first" }`.

- [ ] **R2 — Idea group chat read**: `GET /api/ideas/{idea_id}/chat?limit=50&offset=0` returns a paginated list of message objects for that idea, sorted newest-first. Returns 200 with `{ idea_id, messages: [...], total }`. No auth required for reading.

- [ ] **R3 — Threaded replies**: `MessageCreate` gains an optional `parent_message_id` field. When set, `message_service.send_message()` creates a `reply-to-message` edge from the new message node to the parent. `GET /api/messages/{message_id}/replies` returns all direct children (messages with a `reply-to-message` edge pointing to this id), sorted oldest-first.

- [ ] **R4 — @mention parsing**: `message_service.send_message()` calls `parse_mentions(body)` which returns a list of `@handle` tokens found in the body (regex `@([\w-]+)`). The caller's service stores these as a `mentions` property list on the message node. No separate notification record is stored — notification events are derived from the graph at query time.

- [ ] **R5 — Notification: new DM**: `notification_service.unseen_for()` gains a scan pass over graph message nodes where `to_contributor_id == contributor_id` and `created_at > since`. Each match emits `{ kind: "new_dm", entity_type: "message", entity_id: msg_id, body: <first 100 chars>, actor_name: from_contributor_id, created_at }`.

- [ ] **R6 — Notification: thread reply**: When a message node has `parent_message_id` set, `unseen_for()` fetches the parent node and emits `{ kind: "thread_reply" }` if `parent.from_contributor_id == contributor_id` and `msg.created_at > since`.

- [ ] **R7 — Notification: @mention via message**: `unseen_for()` scans message nodes whose `mentions` list contains `author_name` and `created_at > since`, emitting `{ kind: "mention", entity_type: "message", entity_id: msg_id, body: ..., actor_name: from_contributor_id, created_at }`. Deduplication applies as in the existing service.

- [ ] **R8 — Web: idea chat page**: `web/app/ideas/[id]/chat/page.tsx` renders the idea chat using `IdeaChat` component. The page fetches `GET /api/ideas/{id}/chat` on load. `IdeaChat` renders messages oldest-first with a compose box at the bottom. Each message that has replies shows a "N replies" inline link. Clicking opens `MessageThread` as a side panel showing the reply thread. The compose box sends to `POST /api/ideas/{id}/chat` with the caller's contributor_id from session.

## API Contract

### `POST /api/ideas/{idea_id}/chat`

**Request body**
```json
{
  "from_contributor_id": "alice",
  "body": "Has anyone tested the staking flow on mobile? @bob",
  "parent_message_id": null
}
```

**Response 201**
```json
{
  "id": "msg-a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  "from_contributor_id": "alice",
  "to_idea_id": "contributor-messaging",
  "to_contributor_id": null,
  "to_workspace_id": null,
  "subject": null,
  "body": "Has anyone tested the staking flow on mobile? @bob",
  "mentions": ["bob"],
  "parent_message_id": null,
  "read": false,
  "created_at": "2
```
