# Spec: Creator Economy Bridge

## Purpose

Social creators on YouTube, Instagram, Substack, and TikTok hold audiences that the Coherence Network cannot reach through cold outreach. This spec lets creators register a mirror of their external content — title, link, platform, community tags — so their audience can discover it here and click through to the original. Every click is recorded as a bridge read, which counts toward a frequency profile: a vector of resonance weights computed from the creator's community tags and engagement patterns. Creators with overlapping frequency profiles can form communities and define how CC flows among them — weighting engagement, resonance, or contribution however matches their collective values. The algorithm becomes the community's explicit, visible frequency signature rather than a hidden platform formula.

## Requirements

### R1 — Content mirroring

- [ ] **R1a**: `POST /api/creator-bridge/content` accepts `{ title, external_url, platform, creator_handle, community_tags[] }`. Returns `{ content_id, created_at }` with HTTP 201.
- [ ] **R1b**: `platform` must be one of `youtube | instagram | substack | tiktok | other`. Returns 422 on unknown value.
- [ ] **R1c**: `external_url` must be a valid URL (https only). Returns 422 on invalid URL.
- [ ] **R1d**: `GET /api/creator-bridge/content?handle={creator_handle}` returns paginated list of that creator's mirrored content ordered by `created_at` desc.

### R2 — Read tracking (bridge reads)

- [ ] **R2a**: `POST /api/creator-bridge/reads` accepts `{ content_id, reader_id? }`. Increments `read_count` on the content record. Returns `{ content_id, read_count }` with HTTP 200.
- [ ] **R2b**: `reader_id` is optional (anonymous reads count). When provided it must be a known user handle; unknown handles are accepted but not linked.
- [ ] **R2c**: Read events are stored in a `bridge_reads` table/collection with `(content_id, reader_id_or_null, recorded_at)` for future resonance attribution.

### R3 — Frequency profiles

- [ ] **R3a**: `GET /api/creator-bridge/creators/{handle}/frequency-profile` returns `{ handle, dimensions: { tag: weight } }` where each weight is a float in `[0.0, 1.0]`.
- [ ] **R3b**: Dimensions are derived from the union of `community_tags` across all of the creator's mirrored content. Weight = read_count-weighted tag frequency normalised to `[0.0, 1.0]`.
- [ ] **R3c**: `GET /api/creator-bridge/creators/resonant?handle={handle}&limit=10` returns a list `[{ handle, profile_distance }]` sorted ascending by cosine distance between frequency profiles. Returns empty list if the creator has no mirrored content.

### R4 — Creator communities

- [ ] **R4a**: `POST /api/creator-bridge/communities` accepts `{ name, founding_handles[], algorithm: { weights: { engagement: float, resonance: float, contribution: float } } }`. Weights must sum to 1.0 ± 0.001. Returns `{ community_id, created_at }` HTTP 201.
- [ ] **R4b**: `GET /api/creator-bridge/communities/{community_id}` returns community detail: `{ community_id, name, member_handles[], algorithm, total_cc_distributed, created_at }`.
- [ ] **R4c**: `PATCH /api/creator-bridge/communities/{community_id}/algorithm` accepts new weights. Only existing members (by handle in `member_handles`) may call this; non-members receive HTTP 403. Weights must still sum to 1.0.
- [ ] **R4d**: `GET /api/creator-bridge/communities/{community_id}/earnings` returns `[{ handle, cc_earned }]` summed from resonance events attributed to this community.

### R5 — Resonance earnings

- [ ] **R5a**: When a bridge read is recorded and the content belongs to a community, emit a resonance event weighted by the community's algorithm (engagement weight × read_count delta, r
```

# Spec: Cross-linked public presences

## Purpose

Coherence Network has 6 public-facing surfaces: the GitHub repository, the npm CLI package, the npm MCP server package, the OpenClaw Skill, the live API docs, and the web app. A visitor landing on any one of them should be able to find all the others. Currently the GitHub README's ecosystem table is missing the GitHub row itself, the CLI and MCP READMEs reference a deprecated binary name (`cc help`), and there is no automated check that enforces completeness. This spec fixes the gaps and adds a verification script so regressions are caught at CI time.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1**: `docs/shared/ecosystem-table.md` — add a GitHub row:
  `| **GitHub** | Source code, specs, issues, and contribution tracking | [github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network) |`
  This fragment is included into `README.template.md` via the build script, so the fix propagates to the GitHub homepage automatically on next build.

- [ ] **R2**: `cli/README.template.md` — in the ecosystem table, update the CLI self-row description from `` `npm i -g coherence-cli` then `cc help` `` to `` `npm i -g coherence-cli` then `coh help` `` (the `cc` binary was retired in v0.13.0).

- [ ] **R3**: `mcp-server/README.template.md` — same `cc help` → `coh help` fix in the CLI row of the ecosystem table.

- [ ] **R4**: `api/app/main.py` — verify the `description=` string passed to `FastAPI(...)` at line ~347 contains links to all 6 canonical URLs. If any are missing, add them. Current string already contains Web, API Docs, CLI, MCP Server, GitHub, OpenClaw — verify it is complete and accurate.

- [ ] **R5**: `scripts/build_readmes.py` — run this script after all template edits to regenerate `README.md`, `cli/README.md`, `mcp-server/README.md`, and `skills/coherence-network/SKILL.md`. Commit the regenerated files together with the template changes.

- [ ] **R6**: `scripts/verify_cross_links.py` (new file) — stdlib-only script that:
  1. Defines the 6 canonical presence URLs (as constants).
  2. For each rendered output file (`README.md`, `cli/README.md`, `mcp-server/README.md`, `skills/coherence-network/SKILL.md`) and the API description string in `api/app/main.py`, checks that all 6 URLs appear.
  3. Prints a per-file pass/fail table.
  4. Exits with code 1 if any file is missing any URL; exits 0 if all pass.

## Canonical Presence URLs

| Surface | Canonical URL |
|---------|--------------|
| **Web** | `https://coherencycoin.com` |
| **API docs** | `https://api.coherencycoin.com/docs` |
| **npm CLI** | `https://www.npmjs.com/package/coherence-cli` |
| **npm MCP** | `https://www.npmjs.com/package/coherence-mcp-server` |
| **OpenClaw Skill** | `https://clawhub.com/skills/coherence-network` |
| **GitHub** | `https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network` |

Each surface is expected to link to all 6 (including itself where contextually appropriate, e.g. the CLI README does link to its own npm page as "This package").

## Files to Create / Modify

- `docs/shared/ecosystem-table.md` — add GitHub row
- `cli/README.template.md` — fix `cc help` → `coh help` in ecosystem table CLI row
- `mcp-server/README.template.md` — fix `cc help` → `coh help` in ecosystem table CLI row
- `api/app/main.py` — verify/complete the FastAPI description string (no-op if already complete)
- `scripts/verify_cross_links.py` — new verification script
- `README.md` — regenerated output (via `python3 scripts/build_readmes.py`)
- `cli/README.md` — regenerated output
- `mcp-server/README.md` — regenerated output
- `skills/coherence-network/SKILL.md` — regenerated output

## Verification Scenarios

### Scenario 1 — GitHub README contains GitHub link

```bash
grep -c "github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network" README.md
# Expected output: 2 (badge link + ecosystem table row)
```

### Scenario 2 — Deprecated binary name absent from rendered CLI README

```bash
grep "cc help" cli/README.md
# Expected output: (empty — no lines printed, exit code 1 from grep = no match)
```

### Scenario 3 — All 6 URLs present in MCP README

```bash
for url in "coherencycoin.com" "api.coherencycoin.com/docs" "npmjs.com/package/coherence-cli" "npmjs.com/package/coherence-mcp-server" "clawhub.com/skills/coherence-network" "github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network"; do
  count=$(grep -c "$url" mcp-server/README.md)
  echo "$url: $count"
done
# Expected: each line ends with a number >= 1
```

### Scenario 4 — Verification script passes

```bash
python3 scripts/verify_cross_links.py
# Expected: exits 0, prints a table with all PASS rows
```

### Scenario 5 — API docs description contains all 6 links

```bash
python -c "
import ast, sys
src = open('api/app/main.py').read()
urls = ['coherencycoin.com', 'api.coherencycoin.com/docs', 'npmjs.com/package/coherence-cli',
        'npmjs.com/package/coherence-mcp-server', 'clawhub.com/skills/coherence-network',
        'github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network']
missing = [u for u in urls if u not in src]
if missing:
    print('MISSING:', missing); sys.exit(1)
print('ALL PRESENT')
"
# Expected output: ALL PRESENT
```

## How to show whether it is working

The open question — "how can we show this is working and make proof clearer over time?" — is answered by `verify_cross_links.py` becoming a CI step:

1. **Immediate**: run `python3 scripts/verify_cross_links.py` locally after any presence edit; failure is visible before commit.
2. **CI gate**: add `python3 scripts/verify_cross_links.py` to `.github/workflows/thread-gates.yml` as a lightweight presence-check step. This means every PR that touches a README template or `main.py` must pass the cross-link check.
3. **Drift signal**: the verify script exit code is the signal. If a new surface is added or a URL changes, the script fails and a developer must update all 6 tables. This converts an invisible maintenance burden into a hard check.

## Out of Scope

- Adding new presence surfaces (skills.sh, askill.sh, etc.) — the task is to make the existing 6 consist
```

## data-hygiene-db-monitoring

- **Spec file**: `specs/data-hygiene-db-monitoring.md`
- **Canonical attempt**: branch `worker/impl/ux-my-portfolio/task_d65`, tip `e72f1352`, 2026-03-31

### Content

```markdown
# Spec: Data hygiene — DB row counts, growth, and health dashboard

## Purpose

Operational tables (`runtime_events`, automation telemetry, `agent_tasks`, etc.) can accumulate noise or leak retention bugs. This feature exposes **per-table row counts**, **growth rate between samples**, **anomaly alerts**, and a **web dashboard** plus **`cc db-status`** so operators can prove whether data volume is healthy and whether monitoring is active over time.

## Requirements

- [ ] Expose current row counts for the canonical high-volume tables used in production monitoring.
- [ ] Persist periodic samples so **growth rate** (rows per hour and percent change vs previous sample) is computable.
- [ ] Emit **alerts** when growth exceeds configurable thresholds (percentage and absolute delta), with stronger sensitivity for `runtime_events`.
- [ ] Provide **`cc db-status`** CLI output aligned with the API payload.
- [ ] Provide a **web dashboard** at `/data-health` that renders the same JSON for humans.

## Research Inputs (Required)

- `2024-06-01` - [PostgreSQL COUNT(*) performance notes](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-select.html) — full table counts are acceptable for small/medium ops tables; documented as operational cost.
- `2026-03-28` - Internal task `task_0a82df4ffbb461f9` — baseline row counts and motivation (runtime_events volume suspicious for a young system).

## Task Card

```yaml
goal: Ship DB row-count monitoring with growth, alerts, CLI, and dashboard.
files_allowed:
  - specs/data-hygiene-db-monitoring.md
  - api/app/services/data_hygiene_service.py
  - api/app/routers/data_hygiene.py
  - api/app/services/unified_models.py
  - api/app/main.py
  - api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py
  - cli/bin/cc.mjs
  - cli/lib/commands/db_status.mjs
  - web/app/data-health/page.tsx
done_when:
  - GET /api/data-hygiene/status returns tables, counts, growth, alerts
  - cc db-status prints the same summary (or JSON with --json)
  - pytest api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py passes
commands:
  - cd api && .venv/bin/pytest -v tests/test_data_hygiene.py
  - curl -s "$API/api/data-hygiene/status?record=false"
constraints:
  - Do not modify unrelated tests; fix implementation if tests fail.
```

## API Contract

### `GET /api/data-hygiene/status`

**Query**

- `record` (bool, default `false`): when `true`, insert a new sample row per monitored table after computing counts (enables growth on next request).

**Response 200**

```json
{
  "captured_at": "2026-03-28T12:00:00+00:00",
  "tables": [
    {
      "key": "runtime_events",
      "sql_table": "runtime_events",
      "row_count": 46614,
      "previous_count": 45000,
      "previous_captured_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00+00:00",
      "delta_rows": 1614,
      "hours_since_previous": 24.0,
      "growth_rows_per_hour": 67.25,
      "growth_pct_vs_previous": 3.59
    }
  ],
  "alerts": [
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "table_key": "runtime_events",
      "message": "runtime_events grew 25.0% since last sample (+5000 rows).",
      "delta_rows": 5000,
      "growth_pct_vs_previous": 25.0
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "sample_history_rows": 120,
    "insufficient_history": false,
    "health": "degraded"
  }
}
```

`meta.health` is `ok` | `degraded` | `critical` from worst alert severity present.

### `GET /api/data-hygiene/alerts`

Returns `{ "alerts": [...], "captured_at": "..." }` — same alert list as status (no table listing).

## Data Model

```yaml
DataHygieneSampleRecord:
  table_name: string (physical SQL table name)
  row_count: int
  captured_at: datetime (UTC)
```

Samples are append-only; growth compares the latest two samples per table.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `specs/data-hygiene-db-monitoring.md` — this spec
- `api/app/services/data_hygiene_service.py` — ORM model, counts, samples, alerts
- `api/app/routers/data_hygiene.py` — HTTP routes
- `api/app/services/unified_models.py` — import model for metadata registration
- `api/app/main.py` — register router
- `api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py` — service + API tests
- `cli/lib/commands/db_status.mjs` — CLI
- `cli/bin/cc.mjs` — `db-status` command
- `web/app/data-health/page.tsx` — dashboard

## Monitored tables (logical keys)

| key | SQL table |
|-----|-----------|
| runtime_events | runtime_events |
| telemetry_snapshots | telemetry_automation_usage_snapshots |
| agent_tasks | agent_tasks |
| telemetry_task_metrics | telemetry_task_metrics |
| measurements | node_measurement_summaries |
| contribution_ledger | contribution_ledger |

## Acceptance Tests

- `api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py::test_row_counts_returned`
- `api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py::test_growth_computed_after_two_samples`
- `api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py::test_alerts_on_fast_growth`

## Verification Scenarios

### Scenario 1 — Status returns counts (no history)

- **Setup:** API running with empty `data_hygiene_samples` (or fresh SQLite test DB).
- **Action:** `curl -sS "$API/api/data-hygiene/status?record=false"`
- **Expected:** HTTP 200, JSON has `tables` array with six entries, each `row_count` is a non-negative integer, `previous_count` is null or omitted, `alerts` is empty or informational only, `meta.insufficient_history` is true when no prior sample exists.
- **Edge:** Invalid query `record=maybe` treated as false (or 422 for strict validation — document actual behavior).

### Scenario 2 — Growth rate after two samples

- **Setup:** `curl -sS "$API/api/data-hygiene/status?record=true"` twice with at least 1 second between calls (or test inserts two samples via service).
- **Action:** `curl -sS "$API/api/data-hygiene/status?record=false"`
- **Expected:** For tables with stable or known data, `previous_count` matches first sample, `delta_rows` = second minus first, `growth_rows_per_hour` is numeric (not NaN).
- **Edge:** Single sample only — no false critical alerts solely from missing history.

### Scenario 3 — CLI mirrors API

- **Setup:** `API_URL` or hub URL points at running API.
- **Action:** `cc db-status` then `cc db-status --json`
- **Expected:** Human table lists the same logical keys and row counts as `/api/data-hygiene/status`; JSON mode prints parseable JSON including `tables` length 6.
- **Edge:** API unreachable — CLI exits non-zero or prints error (not a stack trace).

### Scenario 4 — Dashboard loads

- **Setup:** Web dev or production with API rewrite configured.
- **Action:** Open `/data-health` in browser (or `curl -sI` for 200 on page).
- **Expected:** Page shows heading "Data health", fetches `/api/data-hygiene/status`, displays row counts and highlights when `alerts` non-empty.
- **Edge:** API error — page shows error string, not blank white screen.

### Scenario 5 — Alert on synthetic spike (pytest)

- **Setup:** Test DB with two samples for `runtime_events` where second is +50% rows vs first above min delta.
- **Action:** Call `evaluate_alerts` or GET status in test.
- **Expected:** At least one alert with `severity` warning or higher including `runtime_events` in message/table_key.
- **Edge:** Zero rows table — no division-by-zero in growth_pct.

## Evidence this idea is working (independent verification)

- **Live API:** `GET https://api.coherencycoin.com/api/data-hygiene/status` returns non-empty `tables` and `captured_at` on a deployed build that includes this spec.
- **Web:** `https://coherencycoin.com/data-health` renders the dashboard (200).
- **Proof over time:** After enabling `record=true` on a schedule (external cron) or manual samples, `growth_rows_per_hour` and `previous_captured_at` populate — stored samples in `data_hygiene_samples` prove the feature is not a one-shot count.

## Verification

- Manual: scenarios above on staging/production after deploy.
- Automated: `pytest api/tests/test_data_hygiene.py`.

## Risks and Assumptions

- **Risk:** `COUNT(*)` on very large tables can be slow; mitigated by scope (ops tables only) and not recording on every request by default.
- **Assumption:** All monitored tables exist in unified DB schema; if
```

# Spec: Data Retention — Tiered Summarization and Off-DB Backup

## Summary

Operational telemetry grows without bound. This spec defines a three-tier retention strategy that keeps recent data hot and fully queryable, compresses older data into daily aggregates, and exports detail to off-DB JSONL backup before deletion. A retention policy config and scheduled cron pass make the system automatic and auditable. Permanent records (ideas, specs, contributions, audit ledger) are never touched.

The core service and API surface already exist in `api/app/services/data_retention_service.py` and `api/app/routers/data_retention.py`. This spec documents what is live, identifies the missing cron scheduler, and specifies the tests needed to verify the system end-to-end.

## Purpose

Every API call, task metric, and friction event lands in the database. Without active retention, these tables accumulate indefinitely, degrading query performance and increasing storage costs. The solution is not to discard data — it is to move it through tiers: hot (full detail, in-DB), warm (daily aggregates in-DB, detail in JSONL backup), cold (weekly aggregates only). The tier thresholds and backup location are operator-configurable. The pass is safe to re-run: backups are append-only, summaries are idempotent, and dry-run mode previews without mutating anything.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Tier thresholds**: The `data_retention` section of `api/config/api.json` governs the three tiers. Defaults: `hot_days=7`, `warm_days=30`, `cold_days=90`, `backup_dir="data/retention-backups"`. The service reads these at runtime (not module import time) via `_get_hot_days()` / `_get_warm_days()` / `_get_cold_days()` helpers so config changes take effect without restart.

- [ ] **R2 — Safe-to-trim tables**: Only the following tables are eligible for trimming: `runtime_events` (hot tier), `telemetry_automation_usage_snapshots`, `telemetry_task_metrics`, `telemetry_friction_events`, `telemetry_external_tool_usage_events` (warm tier). All other tables are excluded.

- [ ] **R3 — Never-delete list**: The following entity types are never trimmed: `ideas`, `specs`, `contributions`, `contributor_identities`, `audit_ledger`, `coherence_credits`, `value_lineage`, `idea_lineage`. The policy endpoint must enumerate this list in its response.

- [ ] **R4 — Summarize before trim**: `run_retention_pass()` calls `build_daily_summaries()` before any delete operations. Each daily summary (count, avg_runtime_ms, error_count, error_rate, top_endpoints) is stored in `telemetry_meta` under key `retention:runtime_daily_summary:<YYYY-MM-DD>`. Existing summaries for a day are returned from cache without recomputing.

- [ ] **R5 — JSONL export**: Before deleting rows, `_append_backup()` writes each row as one JSON line to `data/retention-backups/<table>/<YYYY-MM>.jsonl`. Files are grouped by the row's timestamp month. Parent directories are created if absent. Files are opened in append mode (`"a"`).

- [ ] **R6 — Dry-run mode**: `POST /api/data-retention/run?dry_run=true` returns a preview with `would_export` and `would_delete` counts but performs zero writes, deletes, or file writes. The response must include `"dry_run": true` and `"total_deleted": 0`.

- [ ] **R7 — API surface**: Four endpoints registered under `/api` prefix via `api/app/main.py`:
  - `GET /api/data-retention/policy` — tier thresholds, never-delete list, safe-to-trim list, backup_dir
  - `GET /api/data-retention/status` — live row counts per trimmable table, backup sizes by table (bytes), last-run metadata
  - `POST /api/data-retention/run?dry_run={bool}` — execute or preview a full pass
  - `GET /api/data-retention/summaries/daily?days_back={1-90}` — pre-computed daily runtime_events aggregates

- [ ] **R8 — Cron / scheduled execution**: A scheduler triggers `run_retention_pass(dry_run=False)` daily at a configurable UTC hour. Implementation options (ordered by preference): (a) APScheduler `BackgroundScheduler` started in the FastAPI lifespan, (b) `asyncio` background task with a 24h sleep loop in the lifespan. Config key `data_retention.cron_hour` (int, default `2`) sets the UTC hour. Scheduler logs start/completion at INFO level.

- [ ] **R9 — Audit trail**: `_meta_set("retention:last_run", ...)` persists the result JSON of every live pass in `telemetry_meta`. `GET /api/data-retention/status` includes `last_run` in its response.

- [ ] **R10 — Tests**: `api/tests/test_dat
```

# Spec: DB Error Tracking — Schema Validation, Friction Events, Error Logging

## Purpose

The API currently degrades silently when core database tables are missing: `_check_schema()` logs at WARNING level and `_ensure_db_tables()` catches startup failures with a warning, but neither records a friction event nor surfaces which tables are absent. Operators and monitors have no structured signal that the DB is degraded. This spec promotes those silent paths to ERROR-level logs, adds a `missing_tables` field to the health response, and records high-severity friction events so the degraded state appears in dashboards and alerts.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1** — `_check_schema()` in `api/app/routers/health.py` is refactored to return a tuple `(bool, list[str])` where the list contains the names of any tables that failed the `SELECT 1 FROM {table} LIMIT 1` probe. Failure is logged at `ERROR` level, not `WARNING`.

- [ ] **R2** — `HealthResponse` in `api/app/routers/health.py` gains a `missing_tables: list[str]` field (default `[]`). The `health()` handler passes the result from `_check_schema()` into both `schema_ok` and `missing_tables`.

- [ ] **R3** — When `_check_schema()` detects missing tables it calls `friction_service.append_event()` for each missing table with:
  - `source`: `"db_schema_check"`
  - `event_type`: `"missing_table"`
  - `severity`: `"high"`
  - `detail`: `{"table": "<table_name>"}`
  This call is wrapped in `try/except` so a friction write failure never propagates to the caller.

- [ ] **R4** — `_ensure_db_tables()` in `api/app/main.py` logs at `ERROR` level (not `WARNING`) when `create_all` raises. On failure it calls `friction_service.append_event()` with:
  - `source`: `"db_startup"`
  - `event_type`: `"table_creation_failed"`
  - `severity`: `"high"`
  - `detail`: `{"error": "<exception message>"}`
  Wrapped in `try/except`.

- [ ] **R5** — `test_flow_core_api.py::test_health_and_meta_endpoints` asserts that `schema_ok` and `missing_tables` keys are present in the health response (extend the existing assertion — do not replace it).

## Research Inputs

- `2026-04-26` — `api/app/routers/health.py` audit — `_check_schema()` exists, logs at WARNING, returns bool only; no friction event recorded.
- `2026-04-26` — `api/app/main.py` audit — `_ensure_db_tables()` swallows errors at WARNING; no friction recorded on failure.
- `2026-04-26` — `api/app/services/friction_service.py` — `append_event(FrictionEvent, path?)` is the write path; `FrictionEvent` model has `severity` string field.

## API Contract

### `GET /api/health`

**Response 200 — all tables present**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "schema_ok": true,
  "missing_tables": [],
  "timestamp": "2026-04-26T12:00:00Z"
}
```

**Response 200 — tables missing (degraded, not 5xx)**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "schema_ok": false,
  "missing_tables": ["contributions", "assets"],
  "timestamp": "2026-04-26T12:00:00Z"
}
```

## Data Model

```yaml
HealthResponse (extended):
  schema_ok: bool          # existing field — unchanged semantics
  missing_tables: list[str]  # new field — names of absent core tables

FrictionEvent (existing model, used as-is):
  source: "db_schema_check" | "db_startup"
  event_type: "missing_table" | "table_creation_failed"
  severity: "high"
  detail: dict
```

## Files

- `api/app/routers/health.py` — refactor `_check_schema()` return type; add `missing_tables` to `HealthResponse`; upgrade log level; call `friction_service.append_event()`
- `api/app/main.py` — upgrade `_ensure_db_tables()` log from WARNING to ERROR; call `friction_service.append_event()` on failure
- `api/tests/test_flow_core_api.py` — extend `test_health_and_meta_endpoints()` to assert `missing_tables` key present in health response

## Acceptance Tests

- `api/tests/test_flow_core_api.py::test_health_and_meta_endpoints` — asserts `schema_ok` and `missing_tables` in response
- Manual / integration: drop a core table, restart API, observe ERROR log + friction event with `severity="high"`

## Verification Scenarios

**Scenario 1 — Happy path: all tables present**
```bash
curl -s https://api.coherencycoin.com/api/health | jq '{schema_ok, missing_tables}'
# Expected:
# {
#   "schema_ok": true,
#   "missing_tables": []
# }
```

**Scenario 2 — Degraded path: missing table shows in response body**
```bash
# In a local dev environment, temporarily rename a table:
# psql $DATABASE_URL -c "ALTER TABLE contributions RENAME TO contributions_bak;"
# Restart API, then:
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/health | jq '{schema_ok, missing_tables}'
# Expected:
# {
#   "schema_ok": false,
#   "missing_tables": ["contributions"]
# }
# Restore: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "ALTER TABLE contributions_bak RENAME TO contributions;"
```

**Scenario 3 — Friction event recorded on missing table**
```bash
# After the missing-table restart above:
tail -n 5 api/logs/friction_events.jsonl | jq 'select(.event_type=="missing_table")'
# Expected entry (fields may vary in order):
# {
#   "source": "db_schema_check",
#   "event_type": "missing_table",
#   "severity": "high",
#   "detail": {"table": "contributions"}
# }
```

**Scenario 4 — ERROR log emitted (not just WARNING)**
```bash
# In the same missing-table scenario:
grep "ERROR" api/logs/api.log | grep -i "missing\|schema\|table"
# Expected: at least one ERROR-level line mentioning the missing table or schema check failure
```

**Scenario 5 — Test suite passes clean**
```bash
cd api && python -m pytest tests/test_flow_core_api.py -q
# Expected: all tests pass, no failures
```

## Out of Scope

- Adding new HTTP status codes to `/api/health` — it always returns 200; degraded state is in the body
- Alerting / notification pipelines — friction events feed existing dashboards
- Schema migration or auto-repair — detection only, no remediation
- Changing `/api/ready` — that endpoint has i
```

  - "Inactivity expiry runs on read (lazy expiry), not a background worker"
  - "Signal types are a fixed enum: listen, offer, yes, pass, done"
  - "Jam sessions reference idea_id optionally — they are not required to be idea-scoped"
---

# Spec: Jam Sessions — Wordless Collaboration Rooms

## Purpose

Jazz musicians improvise together without a plan. They read the room, respond to one another, and build something none of them intended alone — all without a task board, a RACI matrix, or a Slack thread. This spec builds the minimal coordination primitive that captures that: a **jam session**, an ephemeral room where contributors arrive, signal presence, and communicate exclusively through a small set of typed non-verbal signals. No chat. No comments. No tasks. Just the pulse of who is here and what they are sensing right now.

The purpose is twofold: give contributors a way to co-create around an idea without the overhead of structured coordination, and generate a time-series signal record that reveals whether and how collaboration is actually happening — making the proof of living collaboration visible over time.

## Summary

A jam session is a lightweight, ephemeral space where contributors join around an idea (or freely) and communicate via five signal types: `listen`, `offer`, `yes`, `pass`, `done`. Sessions have a tempo (`free`, `focused`, `deep`) that sets the expected cadence. Sessions expire after 120 minutes of silence. The signal history is queryable as a time-series, enabling analysis of collaboration patterns — who offers, who listens, where energy concentrates, when sessions end naturally versus fade out.

The web surfaces this as `/jam` (active session browser) and `/jam/{id}` (live session view with member pulses and signal timeline). No typing required anywhere in the flow.

## Signal Vocabulary

| Signal | Musical analogue | Meaning |
|--------|-----------------|---------|
| `listen` | Ear to the room | I am here and attending — no move yet |
| `offer` | Musical phrase offered | I have something to contribute |
| `yes` | Playing along | I am in resonance with what's happening |
| `pass` | Rest | I am stepping back from this moment |
| `done` | Final cadence | My contribution to this session is complete |

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1**: `POST /api/jams` — create a jam session. Body: `{title: str, idea_id: str|null, tempo: "free"|"focused"|"deep"}`. Returns `{id, join_code, title, tempo, idea_id, status: "open", created_at}` with HTTP 201.

- [ ] **R2**: `POST /api/jams/{id}/join` — record a member entering. Body: `{fingerprint: str}`. Idempotent: same fingerprint joining twice returns 200 without creating a duplicate member. Returns `{member_count: int, joined_at: str}`.

- [ ] **R3**: `POST /api/jams/{id}/signal` — record a non-verbal signal. Body: `{fingerprint: str, type: "listen"|"offer"|"yes"|"pass"|"done"}`. Returns `{signal_id: str, type: str, timestamp: str}` with HTTP 201. Auto-joins member if not already joined.

- [ ] **R4**: `GET /api/jams/{id}` — return full session state. Response includes: `id`, `title`, `tempo`, `status` (`open`|`closed`|`expired`), `idea_id`, `member_count`, `signals` (last 5 minutes, most recent first), `created_at`, `closed_at|null`. Lazy-expire sessions with no signals in 120+ minutes (set `status=expired` on read).

- [ ] **R5**: `GET /api/jams/active` — return all `open` sessions (exclude `closed` and `expired`). Each item: `{id, title, tempo, idea_id, member_count, last_signal_at, created_at}`.

- [ ] **R6**: `GET /api/jams/{id}/signals?since=<iso>` — return signal time-series from the given ISO 8601 timestamp forward. Each item: `{signal_id, type, fingerprint_hash, timestamp}`. `fingerprint_hash` is sha256(fingerprint)[:12] — no raw fingerprint exposed.

- [ ] **R7**: `POST /api/jams/{id}/close` — mark session closed. Returns `{status: "closed", closed_at: str, total_signals: int}`. Only callable with a fingerprint that has joined the session.

- [ ] **R8**: Web page `/jam` lists active sessions with tempo badge, member count, and time since last signal. Each card links to `/jam/{id}`.

- [ ] **R9**: Web page `/jam/{id}` shows: member pulse count, signal timeline (scrolling, live-refreshing every 10s), tempo label, idea link if present, and a signal panel with five buttons (no text labels — icons only).

- [ ] **R10**: All signal types validated as enum; unknown type returns 422 with field-level error.

## Data Model

```yaml
JamSession:
  id: uuid (pk)
  title: string (max 120 chars)
  idea_id: string | null
  tempo: "free" | "focused" | "deep"
  status: "open" | "closed" | "expired"
  join_code: string (6 chars, alphanumeric)
  created_at: datetime (UTC)
  closed_at: datetime | null
  last_signal_at: datetime | null

JamMember:
  id: uuid (pk)
  session_id: fk JamSession
  fingerprint: string (hashed at rest)
  joined_at: datetime (UTC)

JamSignal:
  id: uuid (pk)
  session_id: fk JamSession
  member_id: fk JamMember
  type: enum (listen | offer | yes | pass | done)
  timestamp: datetime (UTC)
```

Inactivity expiry: on any read of a session, if `last_signal_at` is older than 120 minutes and `status == "open"`, se
```

# Spec: Documentation & Developer Experience

## Purpose

A new contributor should be able to find every answer they need — what terms mean, how to set up a working environment, what to do when something breaks, how to add a translation — without asking anyone. This spec creates and consolidates that layer: a canonical glossary, a troubleshooting reference, a setup guide with common error scenarios, an i18n contribution guide, and a link-validation script that keeps internal doc links from rotting.

The open question this spec addresses: how do we show whether the documentation is working? The answer is a concrete link validator that runs in CI — green CI means docs are self-consistent.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1: Glossary (docs/GLOSSARY.md and .claude/GLOSSARY.md)**
  - Every canonical term used in specs, ideas, and CLAUDE.md appears in `docs/GLOSSARY.md` with a one-to-three-line definition and a `Source:` cross-reference to the file or spec where the term is authoritative.
  - Minimum 15 terms. Terms to include (expand or add): `coherence score`, `spec`, `idea`, `task`, `worktree`, `agent runner`, `project manager`, `pipeline`, `holdout tests`, `spec-driven`, `heal`, `i18n`, `locale`, `translation lens`, `SUPPORTED_LOCALES`, `CC (Coherence Credit)`, `friction event`.
  - `.claude/GLOSSARY.md` must mirror the same set with the same definitions (agents load the `.claude/` version; humans use `docs/`).
  - File size of `docs/GLOSSARY.md` must stay under 16 KB after additions.

- [ ] **R2: Setup troubleshooting (docs/SETUP.md)**
  - A "Troubleshooting" section is added after the existing setup steps.
  - It must cover exactly these 5 scenarios, each with symptom, cause, and fix:
    1. **venv activation fails** — `source .venv/bin/activate: No such file or directory` — venv not created; run `python -m venv .venv` first.
    2. **DB connection refused** — `asyncpg.exceptions.ConnectionRefusedError` — PostgreSQL not running or wrong DATABASE_URL; check `docker compose ps` and keystore.
    3. **Port conflict** — `uvicorn: address already in use` — another process on 8000; `lsof -i:8000` then kill or use `--port 8001`.
    4. **Missing API key** — `KeyError: 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'` — key not in keystore `~/.coherence-network/keys.json`; run `coh config-set` or add manually.
    5. **Node version mismatch** — `error: ... requires Node >=20` — use `nvm use 20` or `volta install node@20`.

- [ ] **R3: Troubleshooting reference (.claude/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)**
  - 8+ entries organized into sections: API, Database, Tests, i18n, Agent runner.
  - Each entry has: symptom line, one-line cause, fix command block.
  - Entries must include: API 404 on known route, asyncpg refused connection, pytest fails with "no module named app", i18n `--fill` rate-limit error, agent runner "no tasks" loop, Docker daemon not running, `coh` command not found, test DB isolation failure.

- [ ] **R4: i18n contributor guide (docs/SETUP.md or docs/I18N.md)**
  - A section titled "Adding a new locale" explains the four steps:
    1. Add the locale code to `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `api/app/services/translator_service.py`.
    2. Add display name and flag emoji in the locale list returned by `GET /api/locales`.
    3. Run `python scripts/sync_kb_to_db.py --all` to queue translations for existing concepts.
    4. Verify with `curl /api/locales` — new locale appears in the list.
  - The guide references the `multilingual-web` spec for the product behavior; this section covers only the plumbing.

- [ ] **R5: Documentation navigation (README.template.md)**
  - A `## Documentation` table is added (or updated if present) with 4 rows: Glossary, Setup & Troubleshooting, Contributing, Agent Troubleshooting — each with a path and one-line description.

- [ ] **R6: Link validator (scripts/validate_doc_links.py)**
  - Script reads all `.md` files under the repo root (excluding `.git/` and `node_modules/`).
  - Extracts all `[text](path)` markdown links where path does not start with `http`.
  - Resolves each relative link against the file's directory.
  - Exits 0 if all targets exist; exits non-zero and prints each broken path if any are missing.
  - Accepts `--path` argument to restrict scan to a subdirectory.
  - Skips anchor-only links (`#section`).

## API Contract

No new API routes. The existing `GET /api/locales` endpoint (documented in `api/app/routers/locales.py`) is referenced in the i18n guide but not modified.

## Data Model

No database changes.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/GLOSSARY.md` — expand to 15+ terms, each with `Source:` cross-reference
- `.claude/GLOSSARY.md` — mirror of docs/GLOSSARY.md for agent context
- `docs/SETUP.md` — add Troubleshooting section (5 scenarios) and "Adding a new locale" section
- `.claude/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` — expand to 8+ entries across 5 sections
- `README.template.md` — add/update `## Documentation` table with 4 doc links
- `scripts/validate_doc_links.py` — create link validator script (no external deps, stdlib only)

## Acceptance Tests

No pytest tests for document
```

### Content

```markdown
# Spec: Geolocation awareness — nearby contributors, local ideas, regional news

## Summary

Coherence Network should make **place** a first-class lens: contributors can share coarse location safely, the API resolves human-readable places to coordinates via a **borrowed GeocodingService pattern** (OpenCage primary, OpenStreetMap Nominatim secondary, graceful degradation), and clients can **discover nearby collaborators**, **ideas tied to local contributors**, **regional news**, and eventually **tasks** scoped by geography. Today the API already exposes city-level contributor storage, haversine **nearby** search, and **keyword-based local news resonance**; this spec closes the gap to production-grade geocoding, richer profile surfacing, task proximity filters, web discovery surfaces, and **observable proof** that the feature works and improves over time.

## Purpose

- **Who benefits**: Contributors seeking local collaboration; operators proving regional engagement; reviewers validating deploys with concrete curl scenarios.
- **Failure avoided**: Silent “dead” location features — mitigated via explicit health, metrics, and versioned geocoder behavior.

## Current state (baseline)

The following already exist under `prefix="/api"`:

| Capability | Method | Path |
|------------|--------|------|
| Set location | `PATCH` | `/api/contributors/{contributor_id}/location` |
| Read location | `GET` | `/api/contributors/{contributor_id}/location` |
| Remove location | `DELETE` | `/api/contributors/{contributor_id}/location` |
| Nearby contributors + ideas | `GET` | `/api/nearby?lat=&lon=&radius_km=&limit=` |
| Local news (text resonance) | `GET` | `/api/news/resonance/local?location=&limit=` |

Implementation references: `api/app/routers/geolocation.py`, `api/app/services/geolocation_service.py`, `api/app/models/geolocation.py`.

**Gap**: Forward geocoding (address/city → lat/lon) is not yet centralized as a Living Codex–style **GeocodingService** with OpenCage + Nominatim + fallback. Contributor **GET** responses elsewhere may not yet embed `geo_location`. **Tasks** are not filtered by proximity. **Web** has no dedicated nearby/explore page.

## Requirements

### Functional

- [ ] **R1 — Geocoding adapter**: Port or re-implement the Living Codex **GeocodingService** contract: try **OpenCage** when `OPENCAGE_API_KEY` is set; on failure or rate limit, fall back to **Nominatim** (respecting [Usage Policy](https://operations.osmfoundation.org/policies/nominatim/): User-Agent, rate limits); return structured `{ latitude, longitude, label, source, confidence }` or a typed error. No silent substitution of wrong city.
- [ ] **R2 — Profile enrichment**: Contributor read models used by API consumers (e.g. `GET /api/contributors/{id}` if present, portfolio surfaces, briefs) include **optional** `location_summary` (city/region/country only) when visibility allows; never expose raw precision beyond stored rounding (two decimal places ≈ ~1 km).
- [ ] **R3 — Ideas**: `GET /api/ideas` (or dedicated sub-resource) supports `near_lat`, `near_lon`, `radius_km` **or** `location_query` (string resolved via GeocodingService) to filter/rank ideas by contributor geography (ideas inherit contributor location when author is linked; optional future: per-idea pin).
- [ ] **R4 — News**: Extend regional news beyond keyword overlap: optional path `GET /api/news/resonance/proximity?lat=&lon=&radius_km=` that intersects ingested articles with geocoded datelines/places when metadata exists; keep existing `.../local` for text-only fallback.
- [ ] **R5 — Tasks**: Agent/task listing endpoints support the same proximity filters (`near_lat`, `near_lon`, `radius_km`) where task records carry assignee/creator contributor id and contributor has public/contributors_only location.
- [ ] **R6 — Nearby collaborators UX**: Web exposes at least **`/explore/nearby`** (or `/collaborate/nearby`) with browser geolocation (opt-in) or manual city search, calling `GET /api/nearby` and rendering contributor + idea cards.

### Non-functional

- [ ] **N1 — Privacy**: Default visibility remains `contributors_only`; `private` excludes from all proximity and public profile fields.
- [ ] **N2 — Rate limits**: Geocoding calls are cached (normalized query → result TTL, e.g. 24h) to protect providers and cost.
- [ ] **N3 — Observability**: Expose proof signals (see **Proof and improvement over time** below).

## API changes (target contract)

### New or extended endpoints

| Method | Path | Role |
|--------|------|------|
| `POST` | `/api/geocode` | Body: `{ "query": "Boulder, CO" }` → resolved coordinates + label (auth optional per policy; may be public read with rate limit). |
| `GET` | `/api/health/geolocation` | Returns geocoder configuration status (which backends available, **not** secrets), cache stats sample, last successful resolve timestamp. |
| `GET` | `/api/ideas` | Query params: `near_lat`, `near_lon`, `radius_km` (and/or `location_query`). |
| `GET` | `/api/agent/tasks` (or tasks list) | Query params: `near_lat`, `near_lon`, `radius_km` — filter tasks whose creator/assignee contributor is within radius. |
| `GET` | `/api/news/resonance/proximity` | Optional; requires article place metadata or geocoded tags. |

**Existing endpoints to keep stable** (backward compatible):  
`PATCH|GET|DELETE /api/contributors/{id}/location`, `GET /api/nearby`, `GET /api/news/resonance/local`.

### CLI (if applicable)

- No new `cc` subcommand is **required** for MVP; optional: `cc geo nearby --lat X --lon Y` wrapping `GET /api/nearby` for operator verification.

### Web pages

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/explore/nearby` | Map or list of nearby contributors and ideas; links to profiles and ideas. |

## Data model

### Geocode result (new)

```yaml
GeocodeResult:
  query_normalized: string
  latitude: float
  longitude: float
  label: string
  source: enum [opencage, nominatim, cache]
  confidence: float  # 0.0–1.0 when provided by provider
```

### Contributor (extension)

Existing `geo_location` blob on graph node remains source of truth. Add optional **denormalized** `location_summary` on API DTOs for list views (city, country only).

### Cache (new)

- Key: `sha256(normalized_query + provider_version)`
- Value: `GeocodeResult` + `cached_at` (ISO 8601 UTC)

## Proof and improvement over time (open question)

**How we improve the idea**

- Ship **incrementally**: (1) GeocodingService + `/api/geocode` + health; (2) ideas/tasks filters; (3) web `/explore/nearby`; (4) proximity news when metadata exists.
- Add **feedback loop**: optional client reports (“wrong city”) stored as friction events for manual review — does not auto-change stored coords without user action.

**How we show whether it is working**

- **`GET /api/health/geolocation`**: `opencage_configured`, `nominatim_reachable`, `cache_hit_ratio_24h` (approx), `last_geocode_success_at`.
- **Metrics** (Prometheus-style or existing runtime telemetry): `geocode_requests_total`, `geocode_failures_total{reason}`, `nearby_requests_total`, histogram of `nearby_results_count`.
- **Dashboard / review**: Weekly snapshot: count of contributors with `geo_location`, count of `GET /api/nearby` calls with non-empty results — trending up indicates adoption.

**Clearer proof over time**

- Version field `geolocation_feature_revision` in health payload bumps when behavior changes.
- Commit evidence + spec ID `e165294430dc487b` referenced in release notes.

## Verification Scenarios

### Scenario 1 — Full location CRUD cycle (contributor)

- **Setup**: API running; contributor `contrib-a` exists (create via existing contributor flow if needed). No prior location.
- **Action**:  
  `curl -sS -X PATCH "$API/api/contributors/contrib-a/location" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"city":"Boulder","region":"CO","country":"US","latitude":40.02,"longitude":-105.27,"visibility":"public"}'`  
  Then: `curl -sS "$API/api/contributors/contrib-a/location"`
- **Expected**: First re
```

> **Parent idea (UUID)**: `e7f32e03-2327-4cae-a8d8-facd8281a604` — *What does a neighborhood look like when everyone knows each other?*  
> **Type**: product vision + measurable proof (Living Collective + API read model + web)

# Neighborhood social fabric (knowable, reciprocal, place-bound)

## Summary

This spec captures a **place-bound social fabric** — not a social network: the condition where people on a street (or a small, knowable place) use each other’s **names**, **share meals**, and **entrust one another** with what matters (including watching children). The platform’s job is to (1) **name the pattern** in the Living Collective KB, (2) make **improvement loops** explicit (what to try next), and (3) show **whether the idea is “working”** with **durable, inspectable proof** that can sharpen over time.

“Working” is measured with **public, graph-backed proxies** (recurrent mutual recognition in contributions, co-resonance on the same concept set, and organic sensing/activity around this idea) — not vanity counts. Proof should read like **evidence a thoughtful neighbor would accept**, and improve as we add better edges and longer windows.

## Purpose

- Give humans and the system a **shared vocabulary** for *fabric* vs. *audience* or *scale*.
- Tie the abstract wish (“everyone knows each other”) to **concrete, repeatable actions** the platform can reflect (stories, sensings, specs, co-contribution, mutual concept attention).
- Answer the open product question: **Is this getting stronger, and can we show that clearly next month?**

## Requirements

### R1 — Living story (KB)

- [ ] **R1.1** Add `docs/vision-kb/concepts/lc-neighborhood-fabric.md` with: the feeling of name-knowing, meals, and mutual care; what breaks fabric (anonymity-by-default, turnover-only, fear); what tends it (repetition, invitation, small predictable rituals). Cross-refs: `→ lc-v-living-spaces, lc-intimacy, lc-shared-hold, lc-network, lc-nourishing` (exact renderer pattern from CLAUDE.md / SCHEMA.md).
- [ ] **R1.2** Update `docs/vision-kb/INDEX.md` (and `LOG.md` if your convention requires) so the concept is discoverable and the hierarchy stays honest.

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `docs/vision-kb/concepts/lc-neighborhood-fabric.md` | **Create** — canonical concept story + visuals per SCHEMA |
| `docs/vision-kb/INDEX.md` | **Modify** — link/position new concept |

### R2 — Proof model (read API)

- [ ] **R2.1** Implement `api/app/services/social_fabric_service.py` to compute, for a given `idea_id`:
  - **reciprocity_proxy**: count or score of *mutual* contributor↔artifact or contributor↔contributor **edges** in a rolling window (exact graph query to match existing `graph_service` — implementer chooses minimal sufficient query).
  - **convergence_proxy**: how much distinct contributors co-touch the **same** concept set linked to this idea (e.g. Jaccard-like or count of shared concept IDs above threshold).
  - **sensing_breath_proxy**: count of `sensings` (kinds: breath/skin/wandering/integration) whose `related_to` includes the idea and/or `lc-neighborhood-fabric` (or the idea’s spec/concept set), within a window.
  - **improvement_hints**: 1–3 string hints derived from *which* metric is flat (e.g. “more reciprocity than reach”, “convergence rising but breath quiet”).
- [ ] **R2.2** Add `GET /api/ideas/{idea_id}/social-fabric-proof` returning a **versioned** JSON document (e.g. `schema_version: 1`) with numeric metrics, short `proof_summary` (plain language, ≤ 400 chars), and `window` metadata (e.g. `last_30d` / `all_time` — be explicit in the contract below).

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `api/app/services/social_fabric_service.py` | **Create** — graph-backed metrics + summary |
| `api/app/routers/ideas.py` | **Modify** — new route + Pydantic response model |

**Optional (nice-to-have in same service):** `api/app/routers/world_lens.py` may attach a one-line `fabric_summary` to `GET /api/world/community/{workspace_id}` *only* if the workspace is mapped to this idea without breaking existing clients (additive fields only).

### R3 — Surface (web)

- [ ] **R3.1** On `web/app/ideas/[idea_id]/page.tsx`, when `idea_id` matches this UUID, show a **Social fabric proof** section above the fold on large screens, inline on small screens.
- [ ] **R3.2** Implement `web/components/ideas/SocialFabricProofCard.tsx` that fetches `/api/ideas/{id}/social-fabric-proof` and displays metrics + `proof_summary` with accessible typography (no dense dashboards).

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `web/app/ideas/[idea_id]/page.tsx` | **Modify** — conditionally mount proof card |
| `web/components/ideas/SocialFabricProofCard.tsx` | **Create** — present proof |

### R4 — Operational: KB → DB

- [ ] **R4.1** After KB edits, run `python scripts/sync_kb_to_db.py lc-neighborhood-fabric` (or `--all` if policy requires) and reconcile analogous-to edges per existing KB workflow.

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/sync_kb_to_db.py` | **No change** unless a genuine gap is discovered; prefer CLI invocation |

## API Contract

### `GET /api/ideas/{idea_id}/social-fabric-proof`

**200** — *Example shape (values illustrative)*

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "idea_id": "e7f32e03-2327-4cae-a8d8-facd8281a604",
  "window": { "label": "last_30d", "start": "2026-03-26T00:00:00Z", "end": "2026-04-25T00:00:00Z" },

    "reciprocity_proxy": 0.0,
    "convergence_proxy": 0.0,
    "sensing_breath_proxy": 0
  },
  "proof_summary": "Fabric is still sparse: few mutual edges
```

# Spec: Federation Aggregated Visibility — Proof and Observability

## Purpose

The federation network layer (spec `federation-network-layer`) is implemented and marked done, but it is not yet clear whether aggregated visibility is actually working: are nodes reporting? Is measurement data flowing? Are the dashboard endpoints returning meaningful network-wide data, or empty shells? This spec adds a structured `observability` block to the federation stats endpoints so any operator — human or agent — can verify in one API call that the system is ingesting data as expected. It also adds a config knob for expected node count so the health assessment is grounded, and renders the result on the Nodes web page.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1**: `GET /api/federation/nodes/stats` — new endpoint that returns an `observability` block. Fields: `ingestion_state` (enum: `insufficient` | `partial` | `healthy`), `last_ingestion_at` (ISO 8601 UTC or null), `nodes_reporting_in_window` (int), `expected_node_count` (int from config), `coverage_ratio` (float, `nodes_reporting / expected`), `generated_at` (ISO 8601 UTC). A node "reports" if it has pushed at least one measurement summary within `heartbeat_stale_minutes` of now.

- [ ] **R2**: `GET /api/providers/stats/network` response gains a top-level `observability` key with the same shape as R1. The existing `providers`, `task_types`, `alerts`, `summary`, `nodes`, and `window_days` fields are unchanged.

- [ ] **R3**: `ingestion_state` thresholds: `healthy` when `coverage_ratio >= 1.0`; `partial` when `coverage_ratio > 0`; `insufficient` when `coverage_ratio == 0` (no nodes reported in the window).

- [ ] **R4**: `api/config/api.json` gains a `"federation"` object with keys `"expected_node_count"` (int, default `1`) and `"heartbeat_stale_minutes"` (int, default `30`). `federation_service.py` reads these via `get_int()` (or equivalent config loader pattern already in use).

- [ ] **R5**: `web/app/nodes/page.tsx` adds an `IngestionBadge` component rendered at the top of the Nodes page. It fetches from `/api/federation/nodes/stats` and displays `ingestion_state` with a coloured badge (green = healthy, amber = partial, red = insufficient) plus the `coverage_ratio` as a percentage.

- [ ] **R6**: New test file `api/tests/test_federation_aggregated_visibility.py` covers: (a) empty state returns `insufficient`; (b) one node with fresh measurements returns `healthy` when `expected_node_count=1`; (c) one node with stale measurements returns `insufficient`; (d) one of two expected nodes reporting returns `partial`; (e) `/api/providers/stats/network` observability block is present.

## Research Inputs

- `2026-04-25` — Existing `api/app/routers/provider_stats.py` — `get_network_provider_stats()` already calls `federation_service.get_aggregated_node_stats()`; the observability block is additive
- `2026-04-25` — `api/app/services/federation_service.py` — `FederationNode` ORM model has `last_seen_at`; `FederationMeasurementSummary` ORM model tracks per-node measurement ingestion

## API Contract

### `GET /api/federation/nodes/stats`

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "observability": {
    "ingestion_state": "healthy",
    "last_ingestion_at": "2026-04-25T10:14:00Z",
    "nodes_reporting_in_window": 1,
    "expected_node_count": 1,
    "coverage_ratio": 1.0,
    "generated_at": "2026-04-25T10:20:00Z"
  }
}
```

### `GET /api/providers/stats/network` (updated, additive only)

**Response 200** — existing shape plus:
```json
{
  "providers": { "...": "..." },
  "task_types": {},
  "alerts": [],
  "summary": { "...": "..." },
  "nodes": {},
  "window_days": null,
  "observability": {
    "ingestion_state": "partial",
    "last_ingestion_at": "2026-04-25T09:00:00Z",
    "nodes_reporting_in_window": 1,
    "expected_node_count": 2,
    "coverage_ratio": 0.5,
    "generated_at": "2026-04-25T10:20:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Data Model

```yaml
IngestionStateEnum: "insufficient" | "partial" | "healthy"

ObservabilityBlock:
  ingestion_state: IngestionStateEnum
  last_ingestion_at: string | null   # ISO 8601 UTC
  nodes_reporting_in_window: int
  expected_node_count: int
  coverage_ratio: float              # nodes_reporting / expected_node_count
  generated_at: string               # ISO 8601 UTC

Config (api/config/api.json → federation key):
  expected_node_count: int   # default 1
  heartbeat_stale_minutes: int  # default 30
```

- `api/app/routers/federation.py` — add `GET /api/federation/nodes/stats` endpoint returning `FederationNodeStatsResponse`
- `api/app/services/federation_service.py` — add `get_node_ingestion_state()` function; reads config, queries `FederationMeasurementSummary` for fresh records within `heartbeat_stale_minutes`, returns `ObservabilityBlock`
- `api/app/models/federation.py` — add `IngestionStateEnum`, `ObservabilityBlock`, `FederationNodeStatsResponse` Pydantic models
- `api/app/routers/provider_stats.py` — call `federation_service.get_node_ingestion_state()` in `get
```

> **Parent idea**: [federation-and-nodes](../ideas/federation-and-nodes.md) (absorbed sub-idea)  
> **Relates to**: [federation-network-layer](federation-network-layer.md) (umbrella, marked done) — this spec **narrows** measurement push, persistence, and provable health.

# Federation: Push measurement summaries from nodes to hub

## Summary

Federated **nodes** learn which providers, models, and slots work using **Thompson Sampling** and related local state. That learning must **stay on the node** to preserve autonomy and to avoid shipping high-cardinality or sensitive per-draw data. The **hub** only receives **pre-aggregated measurement summaries** (success/failure counts, value scores, optional error class histograms) over a declared time window for each `(decision_point, slot_id)` row. The hub **stores** these rows in **PostgreSQL** so network-wide **strategy and visibility** features can consume a single durable feed. This spec makes the **contract, boundaries, and proof of operation** explicit so the feature can be judged as “working” and improved over time.

## Requirements

### R1 — Push API (batch)

- [ ] **R1a**: A registered node can `POST /api/federation/nodes/{node_id}/measurements` with a JSON body `{ "summaries": [ ... ] }` where each item matches `MeasurementSummary` (node_id, decision_point, slot_id, period_start, period_end, sample_count, successes, failures, optional mean_duration_s, mean_value_score, error_classes_json).
- [ ] **R1b**: Path `node_id` must equal each summary’s `node_id`; otherwise `422` with a clear `detail` message.
- [ ] **R1c**: `sample_count` must equal `successes + failures` for every summary; otherwise `422`.
- [ ] **R1d**: Response is `201` with `MeasurementPushResponse`: `stored`, `duplicates_skipped`, `duplicates_replaced` (or documented equivalent) so callers can see idempotency.

**Files**: `api/app/routers/federation.py` (handlers), `api/app/models/federation.py` (Pydantic), `api/app/services/federation_service.py` (storage).

### R2 — Hub persistence (PostgreSQL)

- [ ] **R2a**: Each accepted summary is stored in `node_measurement_summaries` with columns including `dedup_key` (unique), `pushed_at`, and metrics fields aligned with the API model.
- [ ] **R2b**: Deduplication uses a deterministic key from `(node_id, decision_point, slot_id, period_start, period_end)`; re-pushes with **higher** `sample_count` may **replace** an existing row; equal or lower sample counts are skipped or not duplicated (see service semantics).
- [ ] **R2c**: Schema creation/migrations are applied via the same path as other federation tables (`_ensure_schema` or migrations).

**Files**: `api/app/services/federation_service.py` (ORM `NodeMeasurementSummaryRecord`, `store_measurement_summaries`, `list_measurement_summaries`), `api/app/services/unified_db.py` (if schema glue lives there).

### R3 — Local vs hub (Thompson Sampling)

- [ ] **R3a**: Full Thompson state (e.g. per-slot Alpha/Beta, internal measurement JSON stores used by `slot_selection_service` / `local_runner`) is **out of scope** for transmission; only rolled summaries go over the wire.
- [ ] **R3b**: Pushing is **asynchronous to selection**: nodes may batch on a schedule or after N tasks; the hub must not assume real-time per-task updates.

**Files (reference, not to duplicate logic on hub)**: `api/app/services/slot_selection_service.py` (Thompson behavior), `api/scripts/local_runner.py` (if push is triggered from runner — integration point to wire in impl).

### R4 — Read + aggregate (proof the hub “has” the signal)

- [ ] **R4a**: `GET /api/federation/nodes/{node_id}/measurements` returns paginated stored summaries (with `total`, `limit`, `offset`) for audit and debugging.
- [ ] **R4b**: `GET /api/federation/nodes/stats` (or a documented equivalent) returns fleet rollups over a configurable time window, including at least a notion of `total_measurements` / node coverage so operators can see that pushes are **accumulating** over time.

**Files**: `api/app/routers/federation.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py` (`get_aggregated_node_stats`).

### R5 — Improving the idea: clarity of “it works”

- [ ] **R5a**: Document in operator-facing material (or a small API field / log line) **at least one** of: (1) count of rows ingested in the last 24h, (2) last `pushed_at` per node, (3) monotonic `total` from list endpoints — so **week-over-week** or **release-over-release** comparison is possible. *Today*, `GET /api/federation/nodes/stats` is already pulled into the automation surface as `networkStats` in `loadAutomationData` (`web/lib/automation-page-data.ts`) — extend or duplicate that pattern if a dedicated “federation learning” view is added.
- [ ] **R5b**: Risks (below) are tracked; if **silent drop** of pushes is possible (auth, network, 4xx), surface friction or counter metrics in `runtime` / `friction` patterns **when** the broader project adds that instrumentation (cross-link only; no scope creep in this spec beyond naming the need).

**Files (optional)**: `web/...` dashboard if one displays fleet stats; `api/app/routers` telemetry endpoints if extended.

## API changes

No breaking changes to the existing measurement push contract are required by this spec; it **formalizes** the current `MeasurementSummary` / `MeasurementPushRequest` / `MeasurementPushResponse` design. If new optional fields are added (e.g. `schema_version`, `source_runner_version`), they mus
```

# Spec: Federation Strategy Propagation (Share optimization strategies across nodes)

## Summary

When one node discovers a better optimization strategy (prompt variant winner, provider recommendation, provider warning), that learning should compound across the federation — but without overriding local reality. This spec adds **strategy pull + bounded network priors** so nodes can weight their local Thompson Sampling with network knowledge, plus **influence + proof surfaces** so we can demonstrate (and improve) whether propagation is actually helping over time.

## Purpose

- Make federation learning compound: improvements discovered anywhere become advisory signal everywhere.
- Preserve local autonomy: each node’s measurements remain the dominant truth as they accumulate.
- Make it provable: provide concrete, queryable evidence that network priors influenced choices and whether that influence improved outcomes.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1 — Strategy pull endpoint returns actionable priors**  
  Add `POST /api/federation/strategies/pull` that returns:
  - active broadcasts (existing strategy types)
  - **computed network priors** for a requested `decision_point` + `slot_ids`
  - metadata needed for auditing: what inputs were used, timestamps, and node-local override threshold applied  
  **Files:** `api/app/routers/federation.py`, `api/app/models/federation.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py`

- [ ] **R2 — Bounded Bayesian priors per slot**  
  Define `NetworkPrior` for each `(decision_point, slot_id)` as bounded Beta parameters:
  - `alpha`, `beta` each in \([1.0, 21.0]\) (cap effective evidence to avoid domination)
  - computed from aggregated federation measurement summaries and/or strategy effectiveness outcomes (implementation chooses exact aggregation, but output must be bounded + stable)  
  **Files:** `api/app/models/federation.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py`, `api/app/db/migrations/add_network_priors.sql`

- [ ] **R3 — Local-first override threshold**  
  Network priors must be ignored for a slot once the node has at least `min_local_samples_for_override` measurements for that `(decision_point, slot_id)`. Default is **20** (exposed in the pull request and reflected in influence).  
  **Files:** `api/app/services/slot_selection_service.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py`

- [ ] **R4 — Thompson Sampling weighting with network priors (advisory)**  
  Extend `SlotSelector.select()` / `_compute_weights()` so a caller can optionally supply network priors for the current decision. The selector must:
  - behave exactly as today when no priors are provided
  - blend priors only for slots under the local override threshold
  - never “force” a selection: still uses Thompson Sampling, just with shaped Beta parameters  
  **Files:** `api/app/services/slot_selection_service.py`

- [ ] **R5 — Influence endpoint (show whether it’s working yet)**  
  Add `GET /api/federation/strategies/influence` to return, for a given `node_id` and decision query:
  - the last pull timestamp
  - the priors applied per slot (alpha/beta)
  - the local sample counts used for override checks
  - the resulting blended parameters (or an explicit “ignored due to threshold” indicator)  
  **Files:** `api/app/routers/federation.py`, `api/app/models/federation.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py`

- [ ] **R6 — Proof endpoint (make proof clearer over time)**  
  Add `GET /api/federation/strategies/proof` that ties:
  - broadcast IDs and their payload hashes
  - measurement summary window(s) used for priors
  - effectiveness outcomes reported by nodes for broadcasts acted upon
  into a deterministic “proof bundle” with a stable `computation_hash` field.  
  **Files:** `api/app/routers/federation.py`, `api/app/models/federation.py`, `api/app/services/federation_service.py`

- [ ] **R7 — DB-backed priors snapshot**  
  Persist computed priors so influence/proof queries are stable and auditable across time, and so nodes can fetch without recomputing on every request.  
  **Files:** `api/app/services/federation_service.py`, `api/app/db/migrations/add_network_priors.sql`

## API changes

### `POST /api/federation/strategies/pull`

Purpose: Node requests network strategies + priors for a specific local decision point.

**Request**

```json
{
  "node_id": "node_1234567890abcd",
  "decision_point": "model_routing",
  "slot_ids": ["openai/gpt-4o-mini", "openrouter/anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"],
  "min_local_samples_for_override": 20,
  "strategy_types": ["provider_recommendation", "prompt_variant_winner"],
  "limit_strategies": 5
```

# Spec: Financial Integration — Fiat Bridge Health, Proof, and Compliance

## Purpose

The fiat bridge described in `financial-integration.md` builds the CC ↔ USDC ↔ fiat exchange mechanics. This spec builds the **proof layer**: endpoints and a public page that make it immediately observable whether the bridge is alive, backed, compliant, and actively converting. Without this layer, the bridge is a black box — contributors cannot trust it, and operators cannot tell when it degrades. With it, the bridge proves itself continuously and anyone can verify.

The open question driving this spec: *How do we show whether the bridge is working, and make that proof clearer over time?* The answer is three endpoints and one public page that turn bridge activity into legible, time-stamped evidence.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1**: Bridge health endpoint. `GET /api/cc/exchange/health` returns real-time status of every subsystem: treasury reserve ratio, exchange rate freshness, on-ramp partner connectivity, off-ramp partner connectivity, KYC provider status, and Base L2 node reachability. Each subsystem reports `ok`, `degraded`, or `down` with a human-readable reason. The overall health is `ok` only when all subsystems are `ok`. Responds in < 500ms — uses cached subsystem checks refreshed every 30s in the background.

- [ ] **R2**: Proof-of-function endpoint. `GET /api/cc/exchange/proof` returns time-series evidence that the bridge has executed real activity. Includes: count of successful swaps, on-ramps completed, and withdrawals completed, each bucketed by day over the requested window (default 30d, max 90d). Also includes the most recent successful swap timestamp, most recent successful on-ramp timestamp, and most recent successful withdrawal timestamp. If any of these timestamps is older than 72 hours, the response includes `"activity_gap": true` for that category — a signal that the bridge may be stalled. This endpoint is publicly readable (no auth required).

- [ ] **R3**: Compliance status endpoint. `GET /api/cc/exchange/compliance` returns current regulatory posture: total KYC-verified contributors, contributors blocked pending KYC, cumulative fiat conversion volume (last 30d) versus KYC threshold, number of conversions that exceeded the threshold and triggered KYC, and KYC approval rate for the last 30d. Also returns the current jurisdictional feature flags (which fiat features are enabled per region). Requires admin auth.

- [ ] **R4**: Bridge metrics persistence. Each successful swap, on-ramp, and withdrawal records a `BridgeMetric` row at completion time: event type, CC amount, USDC amount, fiat amount (if applicable), contributor hash, and a `succeeded: bool`. Metrics are append-only. The proof endpoint queries these rows — not the raw transaction tables — so the proof layer does not add load to the swap hot path.

- [ ] **R5**: Alert conditions logged. When any of these conditions is true, a structured alert is appended to the bridge alert log (`api/logs/bridge_alerts.jsonl`): treasury backing ratio < 1.0, rate stale > 15 minutes, on-ramp partner response time > 5s, off-ramp partner response time > 5s, KYC provider response time > 10s, Base L2 node unreachable. Alerts include timestamp, condition, current value, and threshold. The health endpoint surfaces the most recent unresolved alert per subsystem.

- [ ] **R6**: Public bridge proof page. `GET /bridge-proof` on the web returns a public-facing page showing: treasury backing ratio (live), exchange rate (live), swap count last 30d, on-ramp count last 30d, withdrawal count last 30d, most recent activity timestamps per category, and overall bridge health badge (green / yellow / red). The page pulls from `GET /api/cc/exchange/health` and `GET /api/cc/exchange/proof`. No auth required. Renders as a simple status page that any contributor or potential contributor can bookmark to verify the bridge is live.

- [ ] **R7**: Backing invariant enforced at write time. Before any swap (CC burn) or on-ramp (CC mint) is committed to the database, `check_treasury_invariant()` verifies that `treasury_reserves_usdc >= total_cc_outstanding * exchange_rate`. If the invariant would be violated, the operation is rejected with HTTP 503 and a `TREASURY_INVARIANT_VIOLATION` error code. The health endpoint includes the current invariant state.

## API Contract

### `GET /api/cc/exchange/health`

**Response 200 — all systems ok**
```json
{
  "overall": "ok",
  "treasury": {
    "status": "ok",
    "backing_ratio": 1.23,
    "reserves_usdc": 61500.00,
    "outstanding_cc": 15000000.0,
    "exchange_rate": 0.0041,
    "invariant_holds": true,
    "checked_at": "2026-04-25T10:00:00Z"
  },
  "exchange_rate": {
    "status": "ok",
    "is_stale": false,
    "age_seconds": 142,
    "stale_threshold_seconds": 900,
    "last_computed_at": "2026-04-25T09:57:38Z"
  },
  "onramp_partner": {
    "status": "ok",
    "provider": "coinbase_onramp",
    "latency_ms": 210,
    "checked_at": "2026-04-25T09:59:30Z"
  },
  "offramp_partner": {
    "status": "ok",
    "provider": "moonpay",
    "latency_ms": 185,
    "checked_at": "2026-04-25T09:59:30Z"
  },
  "kyc_provider": {
    "status": "ok",
    "provider": "persona",
    "latency_ms": 320,
    "checked_at": "2026-04-25T09:59:30Z"
  },
  "base_l2": {
    "status": "ok",
    "node": "base-mainnet",
    "block_height": 12345678,
    "checked_at": "2026-04-25T09:59:30Z"
  },
  
```

# Spec: Financial Integration -- CC Fiat Bridge

## Purpose

Contributors earn CC by creating digital assets that others read. Today, CC has no exit to the real economy -- a contributor cannot pay rent with CC. This spec builds the bridge: CC converts to USDC on Base L2, USDC converts to fiat via an off-ramp partner, and fiat lands in a bank account. The reverse path (fiat to USDC to CC) lets new contributors buy into the ecosystem. Without this bridge, CC is play money. With it, CC is income.

The bridge must be trustworthy: every CC is backed by treasury reserves, the exchange rate is a formula (not a knob), treasury operations are on-chain and auditable, and fiat conversion above thresholds requires KYC for regulatory compliance. Contributors must also be able to generate tax documents for their CC earnings.

## Requirements

- [ ] **R1**: CC to USDC exchange via Base L2. Contributors submit a swap request (`POST /api/cc/exchange/swap`). The system burns the specified CC amount, computes the USDC equivalent at the current exchange rate minus spread, and initiates a USDC transfer on Base L2 to the contributor's wallet address. The x402 facilitator infrastructure handles the on-chain settlement.
- [ ] **R2**: Treasury backing with Arweave proof. The treasury maintains a reserve of USDC on Base L2. The total USDC reserve must always be >= total CC outstanding * exchange rate. A proof-of-reserves snapshot is published to Arweave weekly (coordinated with the verification framework spec). The snapshot includes: reserve USDC balance, total CC outstanding, exchange rate, and the Base L2 contract address for independent verification.
- [ ] **R3**: Exchange rate computed from formula only. `rate = treasury_reserves_usdc / total_cc_outstanding`. No manual override. Rate recomputed every 5 minutes from on-chain treasury balance and CC supply ledger. Published at `GET /api/cc/exchange/rate`. The formula, inputs, and output are all visible in the response.
- [ ] **R4**: Fiat on-ramp. New contributors purchase USDC via an on-ramp partner (Coinbase Onramp, MoonPay, or Transak). The USDC arrives at the platform's Base L2 treasury address. The system mints CC at the current exchange rate and credits the contributor's balance. The on-ramp flow is initiated via `POST /api/cc/exchange/onramp` which returns a redirect URL to the partner's hosted checkout.
- [ ] **R5**: Fiat off-ramp. Contributors convert CC to USDC (R1), then request fiat withdrawal via `POST /api/cc/exchange/withdraw`. The system initiates a USDC-to-fiat transfer through the off-ramp partner. Withdrawal to bank account via ACH (US), SEPA (EU), or wire (international). Minimum withdrawal: $10 equivalent. Processing time: 1-3 business days.
- [ ] **R6**: KYC for fiat conversion above threshold. Any fiat conversion (on-ramp or off-ramp) above $2,000 cumulative per rolling 30-day period requires KYC verification. KYC is handled by an external provider (Jumio, Onfido, or Persona). The contributor completes KYC once; the verification status is stored and reused. Below-threshold CC-to-USDC swaps (crypto-to-crypto) do not require KYC.
- [ ] **R7**: Tax reporting API. `GET /api/cc/exchange/tax-report/{contributor_id}?year=2026` returns an annual earnings summary: total CC earned, total CC converted, total fiat received, dates of each conversion, and applicable exchange rates. The response is downloadable as CSV. The platform does not provide tax advice -- it provides data.
- [ ] **R8**: Treasury audit trail on-chain. Every mint (CC creation from USDC deposit) and burn (CC destruction from USDC withdrawal) is recorded as a transaction on Base L2. The transaction includes: CC amount, USDC amount, exchange rate at time of transaction, contributor wallet address (hashed for privacy), and timestamp. The audit trail is queryable via `GET /api/cc/exchange/audit-trail`.

## Research Inputs

- `2026-04-14` - Base L2 documentation (https://docs.base.org) -- EVM-compatible L2 by Coinbase, low fees (~$0.01/tx), USDC native support.
- `2026-04-14` - x402 micropayment protocol (https://www.x402.org) -- HTTP-native payments, facilitator model for settlement. Already integrated in Story Protocol spec.
- `2026-04-14` - Coinbase Onramp SDK (https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/onramp) -- hosted checkout for fiat-to-crypto, supports ACH and card.
- `2026-04-14` - Existing CC economics spec (`specs/cc-economics-and-value-coherence.md`) -- treasury model, supply tracking, coherence score that this spec extends with real USDC backing.
- `2026-04-14` - Persona KYC API (https://docs.withpersona.com) -- identity verification with global coverage, API-first integration.

## Treasury Model

```
                    TREASURY RESERVE (Base L2)
                    ==========================

  Contributor deposits          Platform
  fiat via on-ramp              treasury wallet
       |                        (USDC on Base L2)
       v                             |
  On-ramp partner        +-----------+-----------+
  (Coinbase/MoonPay)     |                       |
       |                 |   USDC Reserve Pool    |
       v                 |                       |
  USDC arrives at        |   Backing invariant:  |
  treasury wallet        |   reserve >= CC * rate |
       |                 |                       |
       v                 +-----------+-----------+
  Mint CC at rate              |
  Credit contributor           |   Weekly Arweave
       |                       |   proof-of-reserves
       v                       v
  CC in circulation      Arweave snapshot:
                         {reserve, outstanding, rate}


  Contributor withdraws
  CC to fiat
       |
       v
  Burn CC at rate
  Compute USDC amount
       |
       v
  Transfer USDC from
  treasury to contributor
  wallet on Base L2
       |
       v
  Off-ramp partner
  converts USDC to fiat
       |
       v
  Bank deposit
  (ACH/SEPA/wire)
```

The treasury is a single USDC wallet on Base L2. All CC in circulation is back
```

*(truncated at 8KB — fetch full content via `git fetch origin 2a83ad71e82f56006c84462411a46a96ece3e71b && git show 2a83ad71e82f56006c84462411a46a96ece3e71b:specs/financial-integration.md`)*
